Paragraph.Text: in any other case, on or before the day that is 90 days after the day of sending of the notice of assessment.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 165(1.1) of the Act after paragraph (c) and before paragraph (d) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: the taxpayer may object to the assessment or determination within 90 days after the day of sending of the notice of assessment or determination, but only to the extent that the reasons for the objection can reasonably be regarded

Section.Text: Subsection 166.1(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Date of objection or request if application granted
Subsection.Text: If an application made under subsection (1) is granted, the notice of objection or the request, as the case may be, is deemed to have been served or made on the day on which the decision of the Minister is sent to the taxpayer.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 169(1) of the Act after paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: but no appeal under this section may be instituted after the expiration of 90 days from the day notice has been sent to the taxpayer under section 165 that the Minister has confirmed the assessment or reassessed.

Subsection.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 180:

Heading.Label: PART I.01
Heading.TitleText: TAX IN RESPECT OF STOCK OPTION BENEFIT DEFERRAL

Section.MarginalNote: Election — special tax and relief for deferral of stock option benefits

Subsection.Text: A taxpayer may make an election in prescribed form to have subsection (2) apply for a taxation year in respect of particular securities if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the taxpayer elected to have subsection 7(8) apply, as that subsection applied before 4:00 p.m. Eastern Standard Time, March 4, 2010, in respect of the particular securities; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the taxpayer has, in the year and before 2015, disposed of the particular securities; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the election under this subsection is filed

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the taxpayer has disposed of the particular securities before 2010, on or before the taxpayer’s filing-due date for 2010, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in any other case, on or before the taxpayer’s filing-due date for the year of disposition of the particular securities.

Subsection.Text: If a taxpayer makes an election under subsection (1) for a taxation year in respect of particular securities, the following rules apply:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 110(1)(d) shall be read without reference to the phrase “1/2 of” in respect of the amount of the benefit deemed by subsection 7(1) to have been received by the taxpayer in the year in respect of the particular securities;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the taxpayer is deemed to have realized a capital gain for the year equal to the lesser of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount that is deductible by the taxpayer under paragraph 110(1)(d), as modified by paragraph (a), and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the taxpayer’s capital loss in respect of the disposition of the particular securities;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the taxpayer is liable to pay a tax for the year equal to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a taxpayer resident in the Province of Quebec at the end of the year, 2/3 of the taxpayer’s proceeds of disposition (as defined in section 54, but determined without reference to subsection 73(1)) of the particular securities, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in any other case, the taxpayer’s proceeds of disposition (as defined in section 54, but determined without reference to subsection 73(1)) of the particular securities;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: to the extent that the taxation year is outside the normal reassessment period (as defined in subsection 152(3.1)), the election is deemed to be an application for reassessment under subsection 152(4.2); and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: notwithstanding subsection 152(4) and as the circumstances require, the Minister shall re-determine the taxpayer’s net capital loss (as defined in subsection 111(8)) for the taxation year and reassess any taxation year in which an amount has been deducted under paragraph 111(1)(b).

Subsection.Text: An amount included under subsection (2)(b) in computing a person’s income under Part I of this Act for a taxation year shall not be included in determining the income of the person for the year under Part VII of the Employment Insurance Act.
Subsection 150(3), sections 150.1 to 152, 155 to 156.1 and 158 to 167 and Division J of Part I apply to this Part with any modifications that the circumstances require.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on March 4, 2010.
The definition adjusted income in subsection 180.2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: adjusted income of an individual for a taxation year means the amount that would be the individual’s income under Part I for the year if in computing that income no amount were

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: included

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: under paragraph 56(1)(q.1) or subsection 56(6),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of a gain from a disposition of property to which section 79 applies, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of a gain described in subsection 40(3.21), or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: deductible under paragraph 60(w), (y) or (z);

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2000 and subsequent taxation years.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 184(3) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Election to treat excess as separate dividend
Subsection.Text: If, in respect of a dividend payable at a particular time after 1971, a corporation would, but for this subsection, be required to pay a tax under this Part equal to all or a portion of an excess referred to in subsection (2) of this section or subsection 184(1) of the Income Tax Act, chapter 148 of the Revised Statutes of Canada, 1952, it may elect in prescribed manner on or before a day that is not later than 90 days after the day of sending of the notice of assessment in respect of the tax that would otherwise be payable under this Part, and on such an election being made, subject to subsection (4), the following rules apply:

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 185.1(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Election to treat excessive eligible dividend designation as an ordinary dividend
Subsection.Text: If, in respect of an excessive eligible dividend designation that is not described in paragraph (1)(b) and that is made by a corporation in respect of an eligible dividend (in this subsection and subsection (3) referred to as the “original dividend”) paid by it at a particular time, the corporation would, if this Act were read without reference to this subsection, be required to pay a tax under subsection (1), and it elects in prescribed manner on or before the day that is 90 days after the day of sending the notice of assessment in respect of that tax that would otherwise be payable under subsection (1), the following rules apply:
Subsection 188(3.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Subsection (3) does not apply to a transfer that is a gift to which subsection 188.1(11) or (12) applies.
Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that end on or after March 4, 2010.
Subsection 188.1(11) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If, in a taxation year, a registered charity has entered into a transaction (including a gift to another registered charity) and it may reasonably be considered that a purpose of the transaction was to avoid or unduly delay the expenditure of amounts on charitable activities, the registered charity is liable to a penalty under this Act for its taxation year equal to 110% of the amount of expenditure avoided or delayed, and in the case of a gift to another registered charity, both charities are jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable to the penalty.

If a registered charity has in a taxation year received a gift of property (other than a designated gift) from another registered charity with which it does not deal at arm’s length and it has expended, before the end of the next taxation year, in addition to its disbursement quota for each of those taxation years, an amount that is less than the fair market value of the property, on charitable activities carried on by it or by way of gifts made to qualified donees with which it deals at arm’s length, the registered charity is liable to a penalty under this Act for that subsequent taxation year equal to 110% of the difference between the fair market value of the property and the additional amount expended.
Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that end on or after March 4, 2010.

Section.Text: Subparagraph 189(6.2)(a)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of all amounts, each of which is an expenditure made by the charity, on charitable activities carried on by it, before the particular time and during the period (referred to in this subsection as the “post-assessment period”) that begins immediately after a notice of the latest such assessment was sent and ends at the end of the one-year period

Section.Text: Subparagraphs 191.2(1)(b)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the day of sending of any notice of assessment of tax payable under this Part or Part I by the corporation for that year,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: where the corporation has served a notice of objection to an assessment described in subparagraph (i), the day of sending of a notice that the Minister has confirmed or varied the assessment,

Section.Text: Paragraph 191.3(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it is filed on or before the day on or before which the transferor corporation’s return for the year in respect of which the agreement is filed is required to be filed under this Part or within the 90-day period beginning on the day of sending of a notice of assessment of tax payable under this Part or Part I by the transferor corporation for the year or by the transferee corporation for its taxation year ending in the calendar year in which the taxation year of the transferor corporation ends or the sending of a notification that no tax is payable under this Part or Part I for that taxation year;

Subsection.Text: The portion of clause 204.81(1)(c)(v)(A) of the Act before subclause (I) is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: if the share is held by the specified individual in respect of the share, a spouse or common-law partner or former spouse or common-law partner of that individual or a trust governed by a registered retirement savings plan, TFSA or registered retirement income fund under which that individual, spouse or common-law partner is the annuitant,

Subsection.Text: Subclause 204.81(1)(c)(v)(D)(II) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: an annuitant under a trust governed by a registered retirement savings plan, TFSA or registered retirement income fund that was a holder of the share,

Subsection.Text: The portion of subparagraph 204.81(1)(c)(vii) of the Act before clause (A) is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: the corporation shall not register a transfer of a Class A share by the specified individual in respect of the share, a spouse or common-law partner of the specified individual or a trust governed by a registered retirement savings plan, TFSA or registered retirement income fund under which the specified individual or spouse or common-law partner is the annuitant, unless

Subsection.Text: Clause 204.81(1)(c)(vii)(C) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: the transfer is to the specified individual, a spouse or common-law partner or former spouse or common-law partner of the specified individual or a trust governed by a registered retirement savings plan, TFSA or registered retirement income fund under which the specified individual or the spouse or common-law partner or former spouse or common-law partner of the specified individual is the annuitant,

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (4) apply to the 2009 and subsequent taxation years.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 207(2)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: may, on sending the notice of assessment for the year, refund without application any allowable refund of the person for the year, to the extent that it was not applied against the person’s tax payable under paragraph (1)(b); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: shall, with all due dispatch, make the refund referred to in paragraph (a) after sending the notice of assessment if an application for it has been made in writing by the person within three years after the sending of an original notice of assessment for the year.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (b) of the definition advantage in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of subparagraph (i), by striking out “and” at the end of subparagraph (ii) and by adding the following after subparagraph (ii):

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a swap transaction, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: specified non-qualified investment income that has not been distributed under the TFSA within 90 days of receipt by the holder of the TFSA of a notice issued by the Minister under subsection 207.06(4); and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (c) of the definition advantage in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a benefit that is income (including a capital gain) that is reasonably attributable, directly or indirectly, to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a deliberate over-contribution, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a prohibited investment in respect of the TFSA or any other TFSA of the holder; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a prescribed benefit.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (b) of the description of C in the definition excess TFSA amount in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a specified distribution;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the description of E in the definition excess TFSA amount in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: nil, if the distribution is a qualifying transfer or a specified distribution, and

Subsection.Text: The definition unused TFSA contribution room in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (a) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: in circumstances where the Minister has, in accordance with section 207.06, waived or cancelled all or part of the liability imposed on the individual, the amount determined by the Minister; and

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (ii) of the description of B in the definition unused TFSA contribution room in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Subsection 207.01(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: deliberate over-contribution of an individual means a contribution made under a TFSA by the individual that results in, or increases, an excess TFSA amount, unless it is reasonable to conclude that the individual neither knew nor ought to have known that the contribution could result in liability for a penalty, tax or similar consequence under this Act.
specified distribution means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a distribution made under a TFSA to the extent that it is, or is reasonably attributable to, an amount that is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an advantage in respect of the TFSA or any other TFSA of the holder,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: specified non-qualified investment income,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: an amount in respect of which tax was payable under Part I by a trust governed by the TFSA or any other TFSA of the holder, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: an amount described in subparagraph 207.06(1)(b)(ii); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a prescribed distribution.

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: revenu de placement non admissible déterminé

Definition.Text: specified non-qualified investment income, in respect of a TFSA and its holder, means income (including a capital gain) that is reasonably attributable, directly or indirectly, to an amount in respect of which tax was payable under Part I by a trust governed by the TFSA or any other TFSA of the holder.

swap transaction, in respect of a trust governed by a TFSA, means a transfer of property (other than a transfer that is a distribution or a contribution) occurring between the trust and the holder of the TFSA or a person with whom the holder does not deal at arm’s length.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (6) apply after October 16, 2009, except that subparagraph (c)(ii) of the definition advantage in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (2), does not apply in respect of income (including a capital gain) earned before October 17, 2009.
The definition deliberate over-contribution in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (7), applies to contributions made after October 16, 2009.

The definition specified distribution in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (7), applies to distributions that occur after October 16, 2009, other than the portion of a distribution that is, or is reasonably attributable to, an advantage that was extended, or income earned, before October 17, 2009.
The definition specified non-qualified investment income in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (7), applies to the 2010 and subsequent taxation years.
The definition swap transaction in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (7), applies to transfers of property that occur after October 16, 2009.
Subsections 207.04(6) and (7) of the Act are repealed.
Subsection (1) applies after October 16, 2009.
Subsection 207.05(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Tax payable in respect of advantage

Subsection.Text: A tax is payable under this Part for a calendar year if, in the year, an advantage in relation to a TFSA is extended to, or is received or receivable by, a holder of the TFSA, a trust governed by the TFSA, or any other person who does not deal at arm’s length with the holder of the TFSA.
Subsection (1) applies after October 16, 2009.
Paragraph 207.06(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: one or more distributions are made without delay under a TFSA of which the individual is the holder, the total amount of which is not less than the total of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount in respect of which the individual would otherwise be liable to pay the tax, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: income (including a capital gain) that is reasonably attributable, directly or indirectly, to the amount described in subparagraph (i).

Subsection.Text: Section 207.06 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

The Minister shall not waive or cancel a liability imposed under subsection 207.05(3) on an individual unless one or more distributions are made without delay under a TFSA of which the individual is the holder, the total amount of which is not less than the amount of the liability waived or cancelled.

The Minister may notify the holder of a TFSA that the holder must cause a distribution to be made under the TFSA within 90 days of receipt of the notice, the amount of which is not less than the amount of the specified non-qualified investment income.
Subsections (1) and (2) apply after October 16, 2009.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 207.06:

Section.Text: A holder of a TFSA shall include in computing the holder’s income for a taxation year under Part I the total of all amounts each of which is the portion of a distribution made in the year that is described in

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subparagraph 207.06(1)(b)(ii);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 207.06(3); or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: subparagraph (a)(ii) of the definition specified distribution.

Section.Text: If an individual is liable to pay an amount of tax under section 207.05 and under sections 207.02 or 207.03 in respect of the same contribution for the same calendar year, the tax payable under section 207.05 for the year shall be reduced by the amount of the tax payable under section 207.02 or 207.03, as the case may be, for the year.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies after October 16, 2009.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 207.07(2)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: may, on sending the notice of assessment for the year, refund without application any allowable refund of the person for the year, to the extent that it was not applied against the person’s tax payable under paragraph (1)(b); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: shall, with all due dispatch, make the refund referred to in paragraph (a) after sending the notice of assessment if an application for it has been made in writing by the person within three years after the sending of an original notice of assessment for the year.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 207.7(2)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: may, on sending the notice of assessment for the year or a notification that no tax is payable for the year, refund without application an amount equal to the amount, if any, by which the refundable tax of the arrangement at the end of the immediately preceding year exceeds the refundable tax of the arrangement at the end of the year; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: shall, with all due dispatch, make such a refund after sending the notice of assessment if application for it has been made in writing by the custodian within three years after the day of sending of a notice of an original assessment for the year or of a notification that no tax is payable for the year.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 212(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (u), by adding “or” at the end of paragraph (v) and by adding the following after paragraph (v):

Paragraph.Label: (w)
Paragraph.Text: a payment out of a trust that is, or was, at any time, an employee life and health trust, except to the extent that it is a payment of a designated employee benefit (as defined by subsection 144.1(1)).

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies after 2009.

Section.Text: Subparagraph 222(4)(a)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if a notice of assessment, or a notice referred to in subsection 226(1), in respect of the tax debt is sent to or served on the taxpayer, after March 3, 2004, on the day that is 90 days after the day on which the last one of those notices is sent or served, and

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 225.1(1.1)(b) and (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an amount assessed under section 188.1, one year after the day on which the notice of assessment was sent; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, 90 days after the day on which the notice of assessment was sent.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 225.1(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a taxpayer has served a notice of objection under this Act to an assessment of an amount payable under this Act, the Minister shall not, for the purpose of collecting the amount in controversy, take any of the actions described in paragraphs (1)(a) to (g) until after the day that is 90 days after the day on which notice is sent to the taxpayer that the Minister has confirmed or varied the assessment.
Paragraph 225.1(7)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: at any time on or before the particular day that is 90 days after the day of the sending of the notice of assessment, 1/2 of the amount so assessed; and

Section.Text: Subsections 244(14) and (15) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Act, where any notice or notification described in subsection 149.1(6.3), 152(3.1), 165(3) or 166.1(5) or any notice of assessment or determination is mailed, or sent electronically, it shall be presumed to be mailed or sent, as the case may be, on the date of that notice or notification.

For the purposes of this Act, if a notice or other communication in respect of a person or partnership is made available in electronic format such that it can be read or perceived by a person or a computer system or other similar device, the notice or other communication is presumed to be sent to the person or partnership and received by the person or partnership on the date that an electronic message is sent, to the electronic address most recently provided before that date by the person or partnership to the Minister for the purposes of this subsection, informing the person or partnership that a notice or other communication requiring the person or partnership’s immediate attention is available in the person or partnership’s secure electronic account. A notice or other communication is considered to be made available if it is posted by the Minister in the person or partnership’s secure electronic account and the person or partnership has authorized that notices or other communications may be made available in this manner and has not before that date revoked that authorization in a manner specified by the Minister.

If any notice of assessment or determination has been sent by the Minister as required by this Act, the assessment or determination is deemed to have been made on the day of sending of the notice of the assessment or determination.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 245(6) of the Act after paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: any person (other than a person referred to in paragraph (a) or (b)) shall be entitled, within 180 days after the day of sending of the notice, to request in writing that the Minister make an assessment, reassessment or additional assessment applying subsection (2) or make a determination applying subsection 152(1.11) with respect to that transaction.

Subsection.Text: The definition employee benefit plan in subsection 248(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: employee benefit plan means an arrangement under which contributions are made by an employer or by any person with whom the employer does not deal at arm’s length to another person (in this Act referred to as the “custodian” of an employee benefit plan) and under which one or more payments are to be made to or for the benefit of employees or former employees of the employer or persons who do not deal at arm’s length with any such employee or former employee (other than a payment that, if section 6 were read without reference to subparagraph 6(1)(a)(ii) and paragraph 6(1)(g), would not be required to be included in computing the income of the recipient), but does not include any portion of the arrangement that is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a fund, plan or trust referred to in subparagraph 6(1)(a)(i) or paragraph 6(1)(d) or (f),

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a trust described in paragraph 149(1)(y),

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an employee trust,

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: a salary deferral arrangement, in respect of a taxpayer, under which deferred amounts are required to be included as benefits under paragraph 6(1)(a) in computing the taxpayer’s income,

Paragraph.Label: (c.2)
Paragraph.Text: a retirement compensation arrangement,

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an arrangement the sole purpose of which is to provide education or training for employees of the employer to improve their work or work-related skills and abilities, or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a prescribed arrangement;

Subsection.Text: The definition retirement compensation arrangement in subsection 248(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (f):

Paragraph.Label: (f.1)
Paragraph.Text: an employee life and health trust,

Subsection.Text: The definition salary deferral arrangement in subsection 248(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (e):

Paragraph.Label: (e.1)
Paragraph.Text: an employee life and health trust,

Subsection.Text: Subsection 248(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: fiducie de soins de santé au bénéfice d’employés

Definition.Text: employee life and health trust has the meaning assigned by subsection 144.1(2);

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (4) apply after 2009.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 38(4)(a) and (b) of the Canada Pension Plan are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: may refund that part of the amount so paid in excess of the contribution on sending the notice of assessment of the contribution, without any application having been made for the refund; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: shall make such a refund after sending the notice of assessment, if application is made in writing by the contributor not later than four years — or, in the case of a contributor who is notified after the coming into force of this paragraph of a decision under subsection 60(7), 81(2), 82(11) or 83(11) in respect of a disability pension, ten years — after the end of the year.

Section.Text: Subsection 85(4) of the Employment Insurance Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The day of mailing or sending, as the case may be, of a notice of assessment described in subsection (2) is, in the absence of any evidence to the contrary, deemed to be the day appearing from the notice to be the date of the notice unless called into question by the Minister or by a person acting for the Minister or for Her Majesty.

For the purposes of this Act, if a notice or other communication in respect of a person or partnership is made available in electronic format such that it can be read or perceived by a person or a computer system or other similar device, the notice or other communication is presumed to be sent to the person or partnership and received by the person or partnership on the date that an electronic message is sent, to the electronic address most recently provided before that date by the person or partnership to the Minister for the purposes of this subsection, informing the person or partnership that a notice or other communication requiring the person or partnership’s immediate attention is available in the person or partnership’s secure electronic account. A notice or other communication is considered to be made available if it is posted by the Minister in the person or partnership’s secure electronic account and the person or partnership has authorized that notices or other communications may be made available in this manner and has not before that date revoked that authorization in a manner specified by the Minister.

Section.Text: Subsection 102(14) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If a notice of assessment has been sent by the Minister as required by this Part, the assessment is deemed to have been made on the day on which the notice is sent.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 152.3(1)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: may refund that part of the amount so paid in excess of the premium on sending the notice of assessment of the premium, without any application having been made for the refund; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: shall make the refund after sending the notice of assessment, if an application for the refund is made in writing by the self-employed person not later than three years after the end of the year.

Subsection.Text: Section 2 of the Universal Child Care Benefit Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: shared-custody parent has the meaning assigned by section 122.6 of the Income Tax Act.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies after June 2011.
Subsection 4(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister shall pay to an eligible individual, for each month at the beginning of which he or she is an eligible individual, for each child who is a qualified dependant of the eligible individual at the beginning of that month,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a benefit of $50, if the eligible individual is a shared-custody parent of the qualified dependant; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a benefit of $100 in any other case.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to payments in respect of months after June 2011.
The definition food waste in subsection 1104(13) of the Income Tax Regulations is repealed.
The definitions biogas, district energy system and eligible waste fuel in subsection 1104(13) of the Regulations are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: biogas means the gas produced by the anaerobic digestion of organic waste that is sludge from an eligible sewage treatment facility, food and animal waste, manure, plant residue or wood waste. (biogaz)

district energy system means a system that is used primarily to provide heating or cooling by continuously circulating, from a central generation unit to one or more buildings through a system of interconnected pipes, an energy transfer medium that is heated or cooled using thermal energy. (réseau énergétique de quartier)
eligible waste fuel means biogas, bio-oil, digester gas, landfill gas, municipal waste, pulp and paper waste and wood waste. (combustible résiduaire admissible)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 1104(13) of the Regulations is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: food and animal waste means organic waste that is disposed of in accordance with the laws of Canada or a province and that is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: generated during the preparation or processing of food for human or animal consumption;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: food that is no longer fit for human or animal consumption; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: animal remains. (déchets alimentaires et animaux)

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (3) apply to property acquired after February 25, 2008, except that the definition district energy system in subsection 1104(13) of Regulations, as enacted by subsection (2), applies to property acquired after March 3, 2010.
Paragraph 1219(1)(f) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: for the drilling or completion of a well for the project, other than a well that is, or can reasonably be expected to be, used for the installation of underground piping that is included in paragraph (d) of Class 43.1 or paragraph (b) of Class 43.2 in Schedule II; or

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to expenses incurred after May 2, 2010.
Section 1402 of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Any amount determined under section 1400 or 1401 shall be determined

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: net of relevant reinsurance recoverable amounts; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: without reference to any amount in respect of a deposit accounting insurance policy.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that begin after 2010.
Section 1406 of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Any amount determined under section 1404 or 1405 shall be determined

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: net of relevant reinsurance recoverable amounts;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: without reference to any liability in respect of a segregated fund (other than a liability in respect of a guarantee in respect of a segregated fund policy); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: without reference to any amount in respect of a deposit accounting insurance policy.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that begin after 2010.
Subsection 1408(1) of the Regulations is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: deposit accounting insurance policy has the meaning assigned by subsection 138(12) of the Act. (police d’assurance à comptabilité de dépôt)

reinsurance recoverable amount of an insurer means an amount reported as a reinsurance asset of the insurer as at the end of a taxation year in respect of an amount recoverable from a reinsurer. (somme à recouvrer au titre de la réassurance)

Subsection.Text: Section 1408 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (7):
A reference in this Part to an amount or item reported as an asset or a liability of an insurer as at the end of a taxation year means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if reporting by the insurer to the insurer’s relevant authority is required at the end of the year, an amount or item that is reported, as at the end of the year, as an asset or a liability in the insurer’s non-consolidated balance sheet accepted by the insurer’s relevant authority; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, an amount or item that is reported as an asset or a liability in a non-consolidated balance sheet that is prepared in a manner consistent with the requirements that would have applied had reporting to the insurer’s relevant authority been required at the end of the year.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) apply to taxation years that begin after 2010.
The definitions Canadian reserve liabilities and reinsurance recoverable in subsection 2400(1) of the Regulations are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: Canadian reserve liabilities of an insurer as at the end of a taxation year means the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of the insurer’s liabilities and reserves (other than liabilities and reserves in respect of a segregated fund) as at the end of the year in respect of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: fire insurance policies issued or effected in respect of property situated in Canada, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (c)
FormulaParagraph.Text: insurance policies of any other class covering risks ordinarily within Canada at the time the policy was issued or effected; and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of the reinsurance recoverable reported as a reinsurance asset by the insurer as at the end of the year relating to its liabilities and reserves in A. (passif de réserve canadienne)

Definition.Text: reinsurance recoverable of an insurer means the total of all amounts each of which is an amount reported as a reinsurance asset of the insurer as at the end of a taxation year in respect of an amount recoverable from a reinsurer. (montant à recouvrer au titre de la réassurance)

Subsection.Text: The description of B in subparagraph (a)(i) of the definition Canadian investment fund in subsection 2400(1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount of the insurer’s Canadian outstanding premiums and policy loans as at the end of the year (to the extent that the amount of the premiums and loans are in respect of policies referred to in paragraphs (a) to (c) of the description of A in the definition Canadian reserve liabilities and were not otherwise deducted in computing the amount of the insurer’s Canadian reserve liabilities as at the end of the year), and

Subsection.Text: Clause (b)(i)(A) of the definition Canadian investment fund in subsection 2400(1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the amount of the insurer’s Canadian outstanding premiums and policy loans (to the extent that the amount of the premiums or loans are in respect of policies referred to in paragraphs (a) to (c) of the description of A in the definition Canadian reserve liabilities and were not otherwise deducted in computing the amount of the insurer’s Canadian reserve liabilities as at the end of the year), and

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (b)(i) of the definition equity limit in subsection 2400(1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount, if any, by which the insurer’s mean Canadian reserve liabilities for the year exceeds 50% of the total of its premiums receivable and deferred acquisition expenses as at the end of the year and its premiums receivable and deferred acquisition expenses as at the end of its preceding taxation year to the extent that those amounts were included in the insurer’s Canadian reserve liabilities for the year or the preceding taxation year, as the case may be, in respect of the insurer’s business in Canada, and

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (a)(ii) of the definition weighted Canadian liabilities in subsection 2400(1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the insurer’s policy loans (other than policy loans in respect of annuities) as at the end of the year, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the reinsurance recoverable reported by the insurer as at the end of the year relating to its liabilities described in subparagraph (i), and

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (b)(ii) of the definition weighted Canadian liabilities in subsection 2400(1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the insurer’s policy loans in respect of annuities as at the end of the year, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the reinsurance recoverable reported by the insurer as at the end of the year relating to its liabilities described in subparagraph (i). (passif canadien pondéré)

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (a)(ii) of the definition weighted total liabilities in subsection 2400(1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the insurer’s policy loans and foreign policy loans (other than policy loans and foreign policy loans in respect of annuities) as at the end of the year, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the reinsurance recoverable reported by the insurer as at the end of the year relating to its liabilities described in subparagraph (i), and

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (b)(ii) of the definition weighted total liabilities in subsection 2400(1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the insurer’s policy loans and foreign policy loans in respect of annuities as at the end of the year, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the reinsurance recoverable reported by the insurer as at the end of the year relating to its liabilities described in subparagraph (i). (passif total pondéré)

Subsection.Text: Section 2400 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (8):

A computation that is required to be made under this Part in respect of an insurer’s taxation year that included December 31, 2010 and that is relevant to a computation (in this subsection referred to as the “transition year computation”) that is required to be made under this Part in respect of the insurer’s first taxation year that begins after that date shall, for the purposes only of the transition year computation, be made using the same definitions, rules and methodologies that are used in the transition year computation.
Subsections (1) to (9) apply to taxation years that begin after 2010.
Paragraphs 2401(2)(b) and (c) of the Regulations are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: shall designate for a taxation year investment property of the insurer for the year with a total value for the year equal to the amount, if any, by which the insurer’s mean Canadian reserve liabilities for the year in respect of its accident and sickness insurance business in Canada exceeds the insurer’s mean Canadian outstanding premiums for the year in respect of that business;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: shall designate for a taxation year in respect of the insurer’s insurance business in Canada (other than a life insurance business or an accident and sickness insurance business) investment property of the insurer for the year with a total value for the year equal to the amount, if any, by which the insurer’s mean Canadian reserve liabilities for the year in respect of that business exceeds 50% of the total of all amounts each of which is the amount, as at the end of the year or as at the end of its preceding taxation year, of a premium receivable or a deferred acquisition expense (to the extent that it is included in the insurer’s Canadian reserve liabilities as at the end of the year or preceding taxation year, as the case may be) of the insurer in respect of that business;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that begin after 2010.
Section 3700 of the Regulations and the headings before it are replaced by the following:
Subsection (1) applies for taxation years that end on or after March 4, 2010.
The portion of subsection 3701(1) of the Regulations before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of the description of B in the definition disbursement quota in subsection 149.1(1) of the Act, the prescribed amount for a taxation year of a registered charity is determined as follows:

Text.Emphasis: Paragraph 3701(1)(b) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: aggregate for each period chosen under paragraph (a) all amounts, each of which is the value, determined in accordance with section 3702, of a property, or a portion of a property, owned by the registered charity, and not used directly in charitable activities or administration, on the last day of the period;

Subsection.Text: Subsections 3701(2) and (3) of the Regulations are replaced by the following:
For the purposes of subsection (1) and subject to subsection (3),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the number of periods chosen by a registered charity under paragraph (1)(a) shall, unless otherwise authorized by the Minister, be used for the taxation year and for all subsequent taxation years; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a registered charity is deemed to have existed on the last day of each of the periods chosen by it.

Subsection.Text: The number of periods chosen under paragraph (1)(a) may be changed by the registered charity for its first taxation year commencing after 1986 and the new number shall, unless otherwise authorized by the Minister, be used for that taxation year and all subsequent taxation years.
Subsections (1) to (3) apply to taxation years that end on or after March 4, 2010.
Subsection 3702(1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of subsection 3701(1), the value of a property, or a portion of a property, owned by a registered charity, and not used directly in charitable activities or administration, on the last day of a period is determined as of that day to be

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a non-qualified investment of a private foundation, the greater of its fair market value on that day and its cost amount to the private foundation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subject to paragraph (c), in the case of property other than a non-qualified investment that is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a share of a corporation that is listed on a designated stock exchange, the closing price or the average of the bid and asked prices of that share on that day or, if there is no closing price or bid and asked prices on that day, on the last preceding day for which there was a closing price or bid and asked prices,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a share of a corporation that is not listed on a designated stock exchange, the fair market value of that share on that day,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: an interest in real property or a real right in an immovable, the fair market value on that day of the interest or right less the amount of any debt of the registered charity incurred in respect of the acquisition of the interest or right and secured by the interest or right, where the debt bears a reasonable rate of interest,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a contribution that is the subject of a pledge, nil,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: an interest, or for civil law a right, in property where the registered charity does not have the present use or enjoyment of the interest or right, nil,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: a life insurance policy, other than an annuity contract, that has not matured, nil, and

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: a property not described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (vi), the fair market value of the property on that day; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of any property described in paragraph (b) that is owned in connection with the charitable activities of the registered charity and is a share of a limited-dividend housing company referred to in paragraph 149(1)(n) of the Act or a loan, that has ceased to be used for charitable purposes and is being held pending disposition or for use in charitable activities, or that has been acquired for use in charitable activities, the lesser of the fair market value of the property on that day and an amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the income earned on the property in the period, and
is the number of months in the period.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies for taxation years that end on or after March 4, 2010.
The definition total reserve liabilities in section 8600 of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: total reserve liabilities of an insurer as at the end of a taxation year means the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total amount as at the end of the year of the insurer’s liabilities and reserves (other than liabilities and reserves in respect of a segregated fund within the meaning assigned by subsection 138(12) of the Act) in respect of all its insurance policies, as determined for the purposes of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions, if the insurer is required by law to report to the Superintendent of Financial Institutions, or, in any other case, the superintendent of insurance or other similar officer or authority of the province under the laws of which the insurer is incorporated, and

is the total of the reinsurance recoverable (within the meaning assigned by subsection 2400(1)) reported as a reinsurance asset by the insurer as at the end of the year relating to its liabilities and reserves in A. (passif total de réserve)

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that begin after 2010.
The Regulations are amended by adding the following after section 9400:

Section.Text: For the purpose of subparagraph 144.1(2)(g)(iii) of the Act, prescribed payments are payments to General Motors of Canada Limited or Chrysler Canada Inc. by the employee life and health trust established for the benefit of retired automobile industry workers by the Canadian Auto Workers’ Union that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: are reasonable in the circumstances;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: are made as consideration for administrative services provided to or on behalf of the trust or its beneficiaries, or as reimbursement for employee benefit payments made on behalf of, or in contemplation of the establishment of, the trust; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the recipient acknowledges in writing shall be included in computing the recipient’s income in the year that they are receivable, to the extent that the recipient deducts in the year, or deducted in a prior year, in computing its income amounts in respect of the services or benefit payments described in paragraph (b).

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies after 2009.
The portion of paragraph (v) of Class 10 in Schedule II to the Regulations before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (v)
Paragraph.Text: property acquired after August 31, 1984 (other than property that is included in Class 30) that is equipment used for the purpose of effecting an interface between a cable distribution system and electronic products used by consumers of that system and that is designed primarily

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in respect of taxation years that end after March 4, 2010.
Class 30 in Schedule II to the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Provision.Text: Property of a taxpayer that is

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: an unmanned telecommunication spacecraft that was designed to orbit above the earth and that was acquired by the taxpayer

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: before 1988, or

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: before 1990

Provision.Label: (A)
Provision.Text: pursuant to an obligation in writing entered into by the taxpayer before June 18, 1987, or

Provision.Label: (B)
Provision.Text: that was under construction by or on behalf of the taxpayer on June 18, 1987; or

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: equipment used for the purpose of effecting an interface between a cable or satellite distribution system (other than a satellite radio distribution system) and electronic products used by consumers of that system if the equipment

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: is designed primarily

Provision.Label: (A)
Provision.Text: to augment the channel capacity of a television receiver, or

Provision.Label: (B)
Provision.Text: to decode pay television or other signals provided on a discretionary basis,

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: is acquired by the taxpayer after March 4, 2010, and

Provision.Label: (iii)
Provision.Text: has not been used or acquired for use for any purpose by any taxpayer before March 5, 2010.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in respect of taxation years that end after March 4, 2010.
Subparagraphs (a)(iii) and (iii.1) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Regulations are replaced by the following:

Provision.Label: (iii)
Provision.Text: heat recovery equipment used primarily for the purpose of conserving energy, or reducing the requirement to acquire energy, by extracting for reuse thermal waste that is generated by equipment referred to in subparagraph (i) or (ii),

Provision.Label: (iii.1)
Provision.Text: district energy equipment that is part of a district energy system that uses thermal energy that is primarily supplied by electrical cogeneration equipment that would be property described in paragraphs (a) to (c) if read without reference to this subparagraph,

Subsection.Text: Subclause (d)(i)(A)(II) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Provision.Label: (II)
Provision.Text: equipment that is part of a ground source heat pump system that transfers heat to or from the ground or groundwater (but not to or from surface water such as a river, a lake or an ocean) and that, at the time of installation, meets the standards set by the Canadian Standards Association for the design and installation of earth energy systems, including such equipment that consists of piping (including above or below ground piping and the cost of drilling a well, or trenching, for the purpose of installing that piping), energy conversion equipment, energy storage equipment, control equipment and equipment designed to enable the system to interface with other heating or cooling equipment, and

Subsection.Text: Clause (d)(i)(B) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Provision.Label: (B)
Provision.Text: it is not a building, part of a building (other than a solar collector that is not a window and that is integrated into a building), equipment used to heat water for use in a swimming pool, energy equipment that backs up equipment described in subclause (A)(I) or (II) nor equipment that distributes heated or cooled air or water in a building,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (d)(iv) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Provision.Label: (iv)
Provision.Text: heat recovery equipment used by the taxpayer, or by a lessee of the taxpayer, primarily for the purpose of conserving energy, or reducing the requirement to acquire energy, by extracting for reuse thermal waste that is generated directly in an industrial process (other than an industrial process that generates or processes electrical energy), including such equipment that consists of heat exchange equipment, compressors used to upgrade low pressure steam, vapour or gas, waste heat boilers and other ancillary equipment such as control panels, fans, instruments or pumps, but not including property that is employed in re-using the recovered heat (such as property that is part of the internal heating or cooling system of a building or electrical generating equipment), is a building or is equipment that recovers heat primarily for use for heating water in a swimming pool.

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs (d)(vii) to (ix) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Regulations are replaced by the following:

Provision.Label: (vii)
Provision.Text: equipment used by the taxpayer, or by a lessee of the taxpayer, primarily for the purpose of generating electrical energy solely from geothermal energy, including such equipment that consists of piping (including above or below ground piping and the cost of drilling a well, or trenching, for the purpose of installing that piping), pumps, heat exchangers, steam separators, electrical generating equipment and ancillary equipment used to collect the geothermal heat, but not including buildings, transmission equipment, distribution equipment, equipment designed to store electrical energy, property otherwise included in Class 10 and property that would be included in Class 17 if that Class were read without reference to its subparagraph (a.1)(i),

Provision.Label: (viii)
Provision.Text: equipment used by the taxpayer, or by a lessee of the taxpayer, primarily for the purpose of collecting landfill gas or digester gas, including such equipment that consists of piping (including above or below ground piping and the cost of drilling a well, or trenching, for the purpose of installing that piping), fans, compressors, storage tanks, heat exchangers and other ancillary equipment used to collect gas, to remove non-combustibles and contaminants from the gas or to store the gas, but not including property otherwise included in Class 10 or 17,

Provision.Label: (ix)
Provision.Text: equipment used by the taxpayer, or by a lessee of the taxpayer, primarily for the purpose of generating heat energy from the consumption of eligible waste fuel, and not using any fuel other than eligible waste fuel or fossil fuel, if the heat energy is used directly in an industrial process, or in a greenhouse, including such equipment that consists of fuel handling equipment used to upgrade the combustible portion of the fuel and control, feedwater and condensate systems, and other ancillary equipment, but not including buildings or other structures, heat rejection equipment (such as condensers and cooling water systems), fuel storage facilities, other fuel handling equipment and electrical generating equipment, and property otherwise included in Class 10 or 17,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (d)(xi) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Provision.Label: (xi)
Provision.Text: equipment used by the taxpayer, or by a lessee of the taxpayer, in a system that converts wood waste or plant residue into bio-oil, if that bio-oil is used primarily for the purpose of generating heat that is used directly in an industrial process or a greenhouse, generating electricity or generating electricity and heat, other than equipment used for the collection, storage or transportation of wood waste or plant residue, buildings or other structures and property otherwise included in Class 10 or 17,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (d)(xiii) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Provision.Label: (xiii)
Provision.Text: property that is part of a system that is used by the taxpayer, or by a lessee of the taxpayer, primarily to produce and store biogas, which property includes equipment that is an anaerobic digester reactor, a buffer tank, a pre-treatment tank, biogas piping, a biogas storage tank and a biogas scrubbing equipment, but not including

Provision.Label: (A)
Provision.Text: property (other than a buffer tank) that is used to collect, move or store organic waste,

Provision.Label: (B)
Provision.Text: equipment used to process the residue after digestion or to treat recovered liquids,

Provision.Label: (C)
Provision.Text: buildings or other structures, and

Provision.Label: (D)
Provision.Text: property otherwise included in Class 10 or 17, or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (d) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Regulations is amended by deleting “or” at the end of subparagraph (xiii), by replacing “and” at the end of subparagraph (xiv) with “or” and by adding the following after subparagraph (xiv):

Provision.Label: (xv)
Provision.Text: district energy equipment that

Provision.Label: (A)
Provision.Text: is used by the taxpayer or by a lessee of the taxpayer,

Provision.Label: (B)
Provision.Text: is part of a district energy system that uses thermal energy that is primarily supplied by equipment that is described in subparagraph (i) or (iv) or would be described in subparagraph (i) or (iv) if owned by the taxpayer, and

Provision.Label: (C)
Provision.Text: is not a building, and

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1), (4) and (8) apply to property acquired after March 3, 2010.
Subsections (2), (3) and (5) to (7) apply to property acquired after February 25, 2008, except that in its application to property acquired before May 3, 2010,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subclause (d)(i)(A)(II) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Regulations, as enacted by subsection (2), shall be read as follows:

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: equipment that is part of a ground source heat pump system that transfers heat to or from the ground or groundwater (but not to or from surface water such as a river, a lake or an ocean) and that, at the time of installation, meets the standards set by the Canadian Standards Association for the design and installation of earth energy systems, including such equipment that consists of underground piping, energy conversion equipment, energy storage equipment, control equipment and equipment designed to enable the system to interface with other heating or cooling equipment, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subparagraphs (d)(vii) and (viii) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Regulations, as enacted by subsection (5), shall be read as follows:

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: above-ground equipment used by the taxpayer, or by a lessee of the taxpayer, primarily for the purpose of generating electrical energy solely from geothermal energy, including such equipment that consists of pumps, heat exchangers, steam separators, electrical generating equipment and ancillary equipment used to collect the geothermal heat, but not including buildings, transmission equipment, distribution equipment, equipment designed to store electrical energy, property otherwise included in Class 10 and property that would be included in Class 17 if that Class were read without reference to its subparagraph (a.1)(i),

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: above-ground equipment used by the taxpayer, or by a lessee of the taxpayer, primarily for the purpose of collecting landfill gas or digester gas, including such equipment that consists of fans, compressors, storage tanks, heat exchangers and other ancillary equipment used to collect gas, to remove non-combustibles and contaminants from the gas or to store the gas, but not including property otherwise included in Class 10 or 17,

Heading.Label: PART 2
Heading.TitleText: AMENDMENTS IN RESPECT OF EXCISE DUTIES AND SALES AND EXCISE TAXES

Subsection.Text: The definition fiscal month in section 2 of the Air Travellers Security Charge Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: fiscal month means a fiscal month as determined under subsection 16(1).

Subsection.Text: Section 2 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: fiscal half-year means a fiscal half-year as determined under subsection 16(2).
fiscal year of a designated air carrier means the same period that is the carrier’s fiscal year under Part IX of the Excise Tax Act.
reporting period means a reporting period as determined under section 16.1.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 5:

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Act, a particular corporation is associated with another corporation if, by reason of subsections 256(1) to (6) of the Income Tax Act, the particular corporation is associated with the other corporation for the purposes of that Act.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Corporations controlled by same person or group
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Act, a person other than a corporation is associated with a particular corporation if the particular corporation is controlled by the person or by a group of persons of which the person is a member and each of whom is associated with each of the others.
For the purposes of this Act, a person is associated with

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a partnership if the total of the shares of the profits of the partnership to which the person and all other persons who are associated with the person are entitled is more than half of the total profits of the partnership, or would be more than half of the total profits of the partnership if it had profits; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a trust if the total of the values of the interests in the trust of the person and all other persons who are associated with the person is more than half of the total value of all interests in the trust.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Act, a person is associated with another person if each of them is associated with the same third person.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 15:

Section.MarginalNote: No action for collection of charge
Section.Text: No person, other than Her Majesty, may bring an action or proceeding against any person for acting in compliance or intended compliance with this Act by collecting an amount as or on account of the charge.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to any action or proceeding that has not, on or before July 13, 2010, been finally determined by the tribunals or courts of competent jurisdiction.

Section.Text: The heading “Fiscal Month” before section 16 and section 16 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The fiscal months of a designated air carrier shall be determined in accordance with the following rules:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if fiscal months of the carrier have been determined under subsection 243(2) or (4) of the Excise Tax Act for the purposes of Part IX of that Act, each of those fiscal months is a fiscal month of the carrier for the purposes of this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if fiscal months of the carrier have not been determined under subsection 243(2) or (4) of the Excise Tax Act for the purposes of Part IX of that Act, the carrier may choose, at the time of registration under section 17, as their fiscal months for the purposes of this Act, fiscal months that meet the requirements set out in subsection 243(2) of the Excise Tax Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if paragraph (a) does not apply and the carrier has not chosen their fiscal months under paragraph (b), each calendar month is a fiscal month of the carrier for the purposes of this Act.

Subsection.Text: The fiscal half-years of a designated air carrier shall be determined in accordance with the following rules:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the period beginning on the first day of the first fiscal month in a fiscal year of the carrier and ending on the earlier of the last day of the sixth fiscal month and the last day in the fiscal year is a fiscal half-year of the carrier; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the period, if any, beginning on the first day of the seventh fiscal month and ending on the last day in the fiscal year of the carrier is a fiscal half-year of the carrier.

Subsection.Text: Subject to this section, the reporting period of a designated air carrier is a fiscal month.

On application in the prescribed form and manner by a designated air carrier, the Minister may, in writing, authorize the reporting period of that carrier to be a fiscal half-year in a particular fiscal year if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the carrier has been registered for a period exceeding twelve consecutive fiscal months;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the total of all charges and amounts collected or required to be collected under this Act by the carrier and any person associated with the carrier in the fiscal year ending immediately before the particular fiscal year did not exceed $120,000;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the total of all charges and amounts collected or required to be collected under this Act by the carrier and any person associated with the carrier in the particular fiscal year does not exceed $120,000; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the carrier is in compliance with the Act.

Subsection.Text: An authorization under subsection (2) is deemed to be revoked if the total of all charges and amounts collected or required to be collected under this Act by the carrier and any person associated with the carrier exceeds $120,000 in a fiscal year, which revocation is effective as of the first day after the end of the fiscal half-year in which the excess occurs.
The Minister may revoke an authorization if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the carrier requests in writing the Minister to do so;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the carrier fails to comply with the Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister considers that the authorization is no longer required.

Subsection.Text: If the Minister revokes an authorization under subsection (4), the Minister shall send a notice in writing of the revocation to the carrier and shall specify in the notice the fiscal month for which the revocation becomes effective.

If a revocation under subsection (4) becomes effective before the last day of a fiscal half-year of a carrier that is authorized under subsection (2), the period beginning on the first day of the fiscal half-year and ending immediately before the first day of the fiscal month for which the revocation becomes effective is deemed to be a reporting period of the carrier.

Section.Text: Subsection 17(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every designated air carrier that is registered or is required to register shall, not later than the last day of the first month after each reporting period of the carrier,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: file a return with the Minister, in the prescribed form and manner containing all prescribed information, for that reporting period;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: calculate, in the return, the total of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: all charges required to be collected by the carrier during that reporting period other than such a charge that was collected by the carrier before that reporting period,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: all amounts each of which is a charge collected by the carrier during that reporting period at a time before the charge became payable under subsection 11(2) if the time at which the charge becomes so payable is after the end of that reporting period, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: all other amounts collected as or on account of charges by the carrier during that reporting period that were not included in a calculation under subparagraph (i) or (ii) for a previous reporting period; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: pay an amount equal to that total to the Receiver General.

Section.Text: Subsection 30(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, on or before the day that is 10 calendar years after the end of a reporting period of a person, or on application by the person on or before that day, waive or reduce any interest payable by the person under this Act on an amount that is required to be paid by the person under this Act in respect of the reporting period.

Section.Text: Subsection 32(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A designated air carrier that has refunded or credited an amount under subsection (1) or (2) within two years after the day the amount was collected and that has issued to a person a document in accordance with subsection (3) may deduct the amount of the refund or credit from the amount payable by the carrier under subsection 17(2) for the reporting period of the carrier in which the document is issued to the person, to the extent that the amount of the charge has been included by the carrier in determining the amount payable by the carrier under subsection 17(2) for the reporting period or a preceding reporting period of the carrier.

Section.Text: Paragraph 33(3)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount was taken into account as an amount required to be paid by the person in respect of one of their reporting periods and the Minister has assessed the person for the period under section 39; or

Section.Text: Section 35 of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a trustee is appointed under the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act to act in the administration of the estate of a bankrupt, a refund under this Act that the bankrupt was entitled to claim before the appointment shall not be paid after the appointment unless all returns required under this Act to be filed for reporting periods of the bankrupt ending before the appointment have been filed and all amounts required under this Act to be paid by the bankrupt in respect of those reporting periods have been paid.
Subsection 39(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If a person has paid an amount assessed under this section in respect of a reporting period and the amount paid exceeds the amount determined on reassessment to have been payable by the person in respect of that reporting period, the Minister shall refund to the person the amount of the excess and, for the purpose of section 28, the refund is deemed to have been required to be paid on the day on which the amount was paid to the Minister.

Section.Text: The portion of section 53 of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Failure to file a return when required
Section.Text: Every person who fails to file a return for a reporting period as and when required under this Act shall pay a penalty equal to the sum of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an amount equal to 1% of the total of all amounts each of which is an amount that is required to be paid for the reporting period and was not paid on the day on which the return was required to be filed, and

Section.Text: Subsection 55(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, on or before the day that is 10 calendar years after the end of a reporting period of a person, or on application by the person on or before that day, waive or cancel any penalty payable by the person under section 53 in respect of the reporting period.

Section.Text: Subparagraphs 72(2.2)(a)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if a notice of assessment in respect of the charge debt, or a notice referred to in subsection 80(1) in respect of the charge debt, is sent to or served on the person, after March 3, 2004, on the last day on which one of those notices is sent or served,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if no notice referred to in subparagraph (i) in respect of the charge debt was sent or served and the earliest day on which the Minister can commence an action to collect that charge debt is after March 3, 2004, on that earliest day, and

Section.Text: Subsections 83(9) and (10) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If a notice or demand that the Minister is required or authorized under this Act to send to a person is mailed, or sent electronically, to the person, the day of mailing or sending, as the case may be, is deemed to be the date of the notice or demand.

For the purposes of this Act, if a notice or other communication in respect of a person is made available in electronic format such that it can be read or perceived by a person or a computer system or other similar device, the notice or other communication is deemed to be sent to the person and received by the person on the date that an electronic message is sent, to the electronic address most recently provided before that date by the person to the Minister for the purposes of this subsection, informing the person that a notice or other communication requiring the person’s immediate attention is available in the person’s secure electronic account. A notice or other communication is considered to be made available if it is posted by the Minister in the person’s secure electronic account and the person has authorized that notices or other communications may be made available in this manner and has not before that date revoked that authorization in a manner specified by the Minister.
If a notice of assessment has been sent by the Minister as required under this Act, the assessment is deemed to have been made on the day of sending of the notice of assessment.

Section.Text: The Excise Act is amended by adding the following after section 36:

Section.MarginalNote: Determination of periods for semi-annual returns

Subsection.Text: The following are six-month periods of a licensed brewer:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the period beginning on January 1 and ending on June 30, or the portion of that period, if any, that ends before the month on which a revocation under subsection (3) or (4) becomes effective; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
