Paragraph.Text: the period beginning on July 1 and ending on December 31, or the portion of that period, if any, that ends before the month on which a revocation under subsection (3) or (4) becomes effective.

Subsection.Text: On application by a licensed brewer in the form and manner specified by the Minister, the Minister may, in writing, authorize the brewer to make a return for each six-month period in a particular year if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the brewer has been licensed for a period exceeding one year;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the total of all duty imposed, levied and collected on beer and malt liquor brewed by the brewer and any person associated with the brewer in the year ending immediately before the particular year did not exceed $120,000;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the total of all duty imposed, levied and collected on beer and malt liquor brewed by the brewer and any person associated with the brewer in the particular year does not exceed $120,000; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the brewer is in compliance with the Act.

Subsection.Text: An authorization under subsection (2) is deemed to be revoked if the total of all duties imposed, levied and collected on beer and malt liquor by the brewer and any person associated with the brewer exceeds $120,000 in a year, which revocation is effective as of the first day after the end of the six-month period in which the excess occurs.
The Minister may revoke an authorization if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the brewer requests in writing the Minister to do so;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the brewer fails to comply with the Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister considers that the authorization is no longer required.

Subsection.Text: If the Minister revokes an authorization under subsection (4), the Minister shall send a notice in writing of the revocation to the brewer and shall specify in the notice the month for which the revocation becomes effective.

Section.Text: Section 37 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every return with respect to quantities, required to be made by this Act, shall be made to the collector on or before the tenth working day of each month for the month last preceding that day.

Despite subsection (1), if a licensed brewer is authorized by the Minister to make a return for a six-month period under subsection 36.1(2), the return shall be made to the collector on or before the tenth working day of the month following the end of the six-month period.

Section.Text: Section 2 of the Excise Act, 2001 is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: fiscal half-year means a fiscal half-year as determined under subsection 159(1.1).
fiscal year of a person means the same period that is the person’s fiscal year under Part IX of the Excise Tax Act.
reporting period means a reporting period as determined under section 159.1.

Section.Text: Section 6 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Act, a particular corporation is associated with another corporation if, by reason of subsections 256(1) to (6) of the Income Tax Act, the particular corporation is associated with the other corporation for the purposes of that Act.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Corporations controlled by same person or group
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Act, a person other than a corporation is associated with a particular corporation if the particular corporation is controlled by the person or by a group of persons of which the person is a member and each of whom is associated with each of the others.
For the purposes of this Act, a person is associated with

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a partnership if the total of the shares of the profits of the partnership to which the person and all other persons who are associated with the person are entitled is more than half of the total profits of the partnership, or would be more than half of the total profits of the partnership if it had profits; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a trust if the total of the values of the interests in the trust of the person and all other persons who are associated with the person is more than half of the total value of all interests in the trust.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Act, a person is associated with another person if each of them is associated with the same third person.

Section.Text: The heading “Fiscal Month” before section 159 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Section 159 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.Text: The fiscal half-years of a person shall be determined in accordance with the following rules:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the period beginning on the first day of the first fiscal month in a fiscal year of the person and ending on the earlier of the last day of the sixth fiscal month and the last day in the fiscal year is a fiscal half-year of the person; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the period, if any, beginning on the first day of the seventh fiscal month and ending on the last day in the fiscal year of the person is a fiscal half-year of the person.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 159:

Subsection.Text: Subject to this section, the reporting period of a person is a fiscal month.
On application in the prescribed form and manner by a person, the Minister may, in writing, authorize the reporting period of that person to be a fiscal half-year in a particular fiscal year if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person is a licensee in one or more of the following categories:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an excise warehouse licensee who does not hold in their excise warehouse manufactured tobacco or cigars,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a spirits licensee,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a wine licensee, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a licensed user;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person has been licensed for a period exceeding twelve consecutive fiscal months;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a category, the total of all duties payable under Part 4 by the person and any person associated with the person in the fiscal year ending immediately before the particular fiscal year did not exceed $120,000;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a category, the total of all duties payable under Part 4 by the person and any person associated with the person in the particular fiscal year does not exceed $120,000;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: in the case where the person is an excise warehouse licensee, the liability of the person and any excise warehouse licensee associated with the person with respect to duty on alcohol entered into an excise warehouse

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: did not exceed $120,000 in the fiscal year ending immediately before the particular fiscal year, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: does not exceed $120,000 in the particular fiscal year;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: in the case where the person is a licensed user, the liability of the person and any licensed user associated with the person with respect to duty on alcohol entered into their specified premises

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: did not exceed $120,000 in the fiscal year ending immediately before the particular fiscal year, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: does not exceed $120,000 in the particular fiscal year;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the volume of absolute ethyl alcohol added to the bulk spirits inventory of the person that is a spirits licensee and any spirits licensee associated with the person did not exceed in the fiscal year ending immediately before the particular fiscal year, and does not exceed in the particular fiscal year, the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the rate of duty on spirits set out in section 1 of Schedule 4;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the volume of wine added to the bulk wine inventory of the person that is a wine licensee and any wine licensee associated with the person did not exceed in the fiscal year ending immediately before the particular fiscal year, and does not exceed in the particular fiscal year, the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the rate of duty on wine set out in paragraph (c) of Schedule 6; and

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: the person is in compliance with the Act.

Subsection.Text: An authorization under subsection (2) is deemed to be revoked if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any of the conditions described in paragraphs (2)(d) to (h) is no longer met in respect of the person, which revocation is effective as of the first day after the end of the fiscal half-year in which the condition is no longer met; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an excise warehouse licensee holds in their excise warehouse manufactured tobacco or cigars, which revocation is effective as of the first day of the fiscal month in which the licensee begins to hold the tobacco or cigars.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may revoke an authorization if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person requests in writing the Minister to do so;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person fails to comply with the Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister considers that the authorization is no longer required.

Subsection.Text: If the Minister revokes an authorization under subsection (4), the Minister shall send a notice in writing of the revocation to the person and shall specify in the notice the fiscal month for which the revocation becomes effective.

If a revocation under paragraph (3)(b) or subsection (4) becomes effective before the last day of a fiscal half-year of a person that is authorized under subsection (2), the period beginning on the first day of the fiscal half-year and ending immediately before the first day of the fiscal month for which the revocation becomes effective is deemed to be a reporting period of the person.

Section.Text: Section 160 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Every person who is licensed under this Act shall, not later than the last day of the first month after each reporting period of the person,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: file a return with the Minister, in the prescribed form and manner, for that reporting period;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: calculate, in the return, the total amount of the duty payable, if any, by the person for that reporting period; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: pay that amount to the Receiver General.

Section.Text: Subsection 170(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If, at any time, a person pays an amount not less than the total of all amounts, other than interest and penalty payable under section 251.1, owing at that time to Her Majesty under this Act for a reporting period of the person and the total amount of interest and the penalty payable by the person under this Act for that period is not more than $25.00, the Minister may waive the total amount.

Section.Text: Paragraph 176(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount was taken into account as duty for a reporting period of the person and the Minister has assessed the person for the period under section 188; or

Section.Text: Section 178 of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a trustee is appointed under the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act to act in the administration of the estate of a bankrupt, a refund or any other payment under this Act that the bankrupt was entitled to claim before the appointment shall not be paid after the appointment unless all returns required under this Act to be filed for reporting periods of the bankrupt ending before the appointment have been filed and all amounts required under this Act to be paid by the bankrupt in respect of those reporting periods have been paid.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 188(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the duty payable by a person for a reporting period of the person; and

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 188(3) of the Act before subparagraph (a)(ii) is replaced by the following:
If, in assessing the duty, interest or other amount payable by a person for a reporting period of the person or other amount payable by a person under this Act, the Minister determines that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a refund would have been payable to the person if it had been claimed in an application under this Act filed on the particular day that is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the assessment is in respect of duty payable for the reporting period, the day on which the return for the period was required to be filed, or

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 188(4) of the Act before clause (b)(i)(C) is replaced by the following:

If, in assessing the duty payable by a person for a reporting period of the person, the Minister determines that there is an overpayment of duty payable for the period, unless the assessment is made in the circumstances described in paragraph 191(4)(a) or (b) after the time otherwise limited for the assessment under paragraph 191(1)(a), the Minister shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: apply

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: all or part of the overpayment

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any amount (in this paragraph referred to as the “outstanding amount”) that, on the particular day on which the person was required to file a return for the period, the person defaulted in paying under this Act and that remains unpaid on the day on which notice of the assessment is sent to the person,

ContinuedParagraph.Text: as if the person had, on the particular day, paid the amount so applied on account of the outstanding amount;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: apply

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: all or part of the overpayment that was not applied under paragraph (a) together with interest on the overpayment at the prescribed rate, computed for the period beginning on the day that is 30 days after the latest of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the particular day,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the day on which the return for the reporting period was filed, and

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 188(4)(c)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the day on which the return for the reporting period was filed, and

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 188(5) of the Act before clause (a)(ii)(B) is replaced by the following:

If, in assessing the duty payable by a person for a reporting period of the person or an amount (in this subsection referred to as the “overdue amount”) payable by a person under this Act, all or part of a refund is not applied under subsection (3) against that duty payable or overdue amount, except if the assessment is made in the circumstances described in paragraph 191(4)(a) or (b) after the time otherwise limited for the assessment under paragraph 191(1)(a), the Minister shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: apply

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: all or part of the refund that was not applied under subsection (3)

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any other amount (in this paragraph referred to as the “outstanding amount”) that, on the particular day that is

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: if the assessment is in respect of duty payable for the reporting period, the day on which the return for the period was required to be filed, or

Subsection.Text: Clause 188(5)(b)(i)(B) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: if the assessment is in respect of duty payable for the reporting period, the day on which the return for the period was filed,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 188(5)(c)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the assessment is in respect of duty payable for the reporting period, the day on which the return for the period was filed,

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 188(6) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

An overpayment of duty payable for a reporting period of a person and interest on the overpayment shall not be applied under paragraph (4)(b) or refunded under paragraph (4)(c) unless the person has, before the day on which notice of the assessment is sent to the person, filed all returns and other records of which the Minister has knowledge and that the person was required to file with
The portion of subsection 188(9) of the Act before subparagraph (a)(iii) is replaced by the following:

If a person has paid an amount on account of any duty, interest or other amount assessed under this section in respect of a reporting period and the amount paid exceeds the amount determined on reassessment to have been payable by the person, the Minister may refund to the person the amount of the excess, together with interest on the excess amount at the prescribed rate for the period that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: begins on the day that is 30 days after the latest of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the day on which the person was required to file a return for the reporting period,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the day on which the person filed a return for the reporting period, and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 188(10) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Meaning of overpayment of duty payable
Subsection.Text: In this section, overpayment of duty payable of a person for a reporting period of the person means the amount, if any, by which the total of all amounts paid by the person on account of duty payable for the period exceeds the total of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the duty payable for the period, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: all amounts paid to the person under this Act as a refund for the period.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 191(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an assessment of the duty payable for a reporting period, more than four years after the later of the day on which the return for the period was required to be filed and the day on which the return was filed;

Subsection.Text: Subsections 191(5) and (6) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: No limitation if payment for another reporting period
Subsection.Text: If, in making an assessment, the Minister determines that a person has paid in respect of any matter an amount as or on account of duty payable for a reporting period of the person that was payable for another reporting period of the person, the Minister may at any time make an assessment for that other period in respect of that matter.

If the result of a reassessment on an objection to, or a decision on an appeal from, an assessment is to reduce the amount of duty payable by a person and, by reason of the reduction, any refund or other payment claimed by the person for a reporting period, or in an application for a refund or other payment, should be decreased, the Minister may at any time assess or reassess that reporting period or that application only for the purpose of taking the reduction of duty into account in respect of the refund or other payment.

Section.Text: Subsection 193(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A notice of assessment may include assessments in respect of any number or combination of reporting periods, refunds or amounts payable under this Act.
Subparagraph 212(2)(a)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the trustee is liable for the payment of any duty, interest or other amount that became payable by the person after the particular day in respect of reporting periods that ended on or before the particular day, or of any duty, interest or other amount that became payable by the person after the particular day, only to the extent of the property of the person in possession of the trustee available to satisfy the liability,

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 212(2)(c) to (e) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the reporting period of the person begins and ends on the day on which it would have begun and ended if the bankruptcy had not occurred, except that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the reporting period of the person during which the person becomes a bankrupt shall end on the particular day and a new reporting period of the person in relation to the activities of the person to which the bankruptcy relates shall begin on the day immediately after the particular day, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the reporting period of the person, in relation to the activities of the person to which the bankruptcy relates, during which the trustee in bankruptcy is discharged under the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act shall end on the day on which the discharge is granted;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: subject to paragraph (f), the trustee in bankruptcy shall file with the Minister in the prescribed form and manner all returns in respect of the activities of the person to which the bankruptcy relates for the reporting periods of the person ending in the period beginning on the day immediately after the particular day and ending on the day on which the discharge of the trustee is granted under the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act and that are required under this Act to be filed by the person, as if those activities were the only activities of the person;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: subject to paragraph (f), if the person has not on or before the particular day filed a return required under this Act to be filed by the person for a reporting period of the person ending on or before the particular day, the trustee in bankruptcy shall, unless the Minister waives in writing the requirement for the trustee to file the return, file with the Minister in the prescribed form and manner a return for that reporting period of the person; and

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 212(3)(c) to (e) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the reporting period of the person begins and ends on the day on which it would have begun and ended if the vesting had not occurred, except that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the reporting period of the person, in relation to the relevant assets of the receiver, during which the receiver begins to act as receiver of the person, shall end on the particular day and a new reporting period of the person in relation to the relevant assets shall begin on the day immediately after the particular day, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the reporting period of the person, in relation to the relevant assets, during which the receiver ceases to act as receiver of the person, shall end on the day on which the receiver ceases to act as receiver of the person;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the receiver shall file with the Minister in the prescribed form and manner all returns in respect of the relevant assets of the receiver for reporting periods ending in the period during which the receiver is acting as receiver and that are required under this Act to be made by the person, as if the relevant assets were the only businesses, properties, affairs and assets of the person; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: if the person has not on or before the particular day filed a return required under this Act to be filed by the person for a reporting period of the person ending on or before the particular day, the receiver shall, unless the Minister waives in writing the requirement for the receiver to file the return, file with the Minister in the prescribed form and manner a return for that reporting period that relates to the businesses, properties, affairs or assets of the person that would have been the relevant assets of the receiver if the receiver had been acting as receiver of the person during that reporting period.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 212(4)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: all duty, interest and other amounts that are payable by the other person under this Act in respect of the reporting period during which the distribution is made, or any previous reporting period; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: all duty, interest and other amounts that are, or can reasonably be expected to become, payable under this Act by the representative or receiver in that capacity in respect of the reporting period during which the distribution is made, or any previous reporting period.

Section.Text: The portion of section 251.1 of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:
Every person who fails to file a return for a reporting period as and when required under this Act shall pay a penalty equal to the sum of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an amount equal to 1% of the total of all amounts each of which is an amount that is required to be paid for the reporting period and was not paid before the end of the day on which the return was required to be filed, and

Section.Text: The portion of section 253 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Every person who knowingly, or under circumstances amounting to gross negligence, makes, or participates in, assents to or acquiesces in the making of, a false statement or omission in a return, application, form, certificate, statement, invoice or answer (each of which is in this section referred to as a “return”) made in respect of a reporting period or activity is liable to a penalty equal to the greater of $250 and 25% of the total of
Section 255.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Waiving or reducing failure to file penalty
Section.Text: The Minister may, on or before the day that is 10 calendar years after the end of a reporting period of a person, or on application by the person on or before that day, waive or reduce any penalty payable by the person under section 251.1 in respect of a return for the reporting period.
Subparagraphs 284(2.2)(a)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if a notice of assessment in respect of the tax debt, or a notice referred to in subsection 254(1) or 294(1) in respect of the tax debt, is sent to or served on the person, after March 3, 2004, on the day that is 90 days after the day on which the last one of those notices is sent or served,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if no notice referred to in subparagraph (i) in respect of the tax debt was sent or served and the earliest day on which the Minister can commence an action to collect that tax debt is after March 3, 2004, on that earliest day, and

Section.Text: Subparagraph 297(1)(e)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an amount that the transferor is liable to pay under this Act in respect of the reporting period in which the property was transferred or any preceding reporting period, or

Section.Text: Subsections 301(9) and (10) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If a notice or demand that the Minister is required or authorized under this Act to send to a person is mailed, or sent electronically, to the person, the day of mailing or sending, as the case may be, is deemed to be the date of the notice or demand.

For the purposes of this Act, if a notice or other communication in respect of a person is made available in electronic format such that it can be read or perceived by a person or a computer system or other similar device, the notice or other communication is deemed to be sent to the person and received by the person on the date that an electronic message is sent, to the electronic address most recently provided before that date by the person to the Minister for the purposes of this subsection, informing the person that a notice or other communication requiring the person’s immediate attention is available in the person’s secure electronic account. A notice or other communication is considered to be made available if it is posted by the Minister in the person’s secure electronic account and the person has authorized that notices or other communications may be made available in this manner and has not before that date revoked that authorization in a manner specified by the Minister.
If a notice of assessment has been sent by the Minister as required under this Act, the assessment is deemed to have been made on the day of sending of the notice of assessment.

Section.Text: Section 2 of the Excise Tax Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2.2):

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Act, a particular corporation is associated with another corporation if, by reason of subsections 256(1) to (6) of the Income Tax Act, the particular corporation is associated with the other corporation for the purposes of that Act.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Corporations controlled by same person or group
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Act, a person other than a corporation is associated with a particular corporation if the particular corporation is controlled by the person or by a group of persons of which the person is a member and each of whom is associated with each of the others.
For the purposes of this Act, a person is associated with

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a partnership if the total of the shares of the profits of the partnership to which the person and all other persons who are associated with the person are entitled is more than half of the total profits of the partnership, or would be more than half of the total profits of the partnership if it had profits; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a trust if the total of the values of the interests in the trust of the person and all other persons who are associated with the person is more than half of the total value of all interests in the trust.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Act, a person is associated with another person if each of them is associated with the same third person.

Section.Text: Subsection 58.1(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: fiscal half-year means a fiscal half-year as determined under subsection 78(1.1).
fiscal year of a person means the same period that is the person’s fiscal year under Part IX.
reporting period means a reporting period as determined under section 78.1.

Section.Text: Section 78 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.Text: The fiscal half-years of a person shall be determined in accordance with the following rules:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the period beginning on the first day of the first fiscal month in a fiscal year of the person and ending on the earlier of the last day of the sixth fiscal month and the last day in the fiscal year is a fiscal half-year of the person; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the period, if any, beginning on the first day of the seventh fiscal month and ending on the last day in the fiscal year of the person is a fiscal half-year of the person.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 78:

Subsection.Text: Subject to this section, the reporting period of a person is a fiscal month.
On application in the prescribed form and manner by a person, the Minister may, in writing, authorize the reporting period of that person to be a fiscal half-year in a particular fiscal year if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person has been required to pay tax under Part III, or has been holding a licence granted under or in respect of that Part, for a period exceeding twelve consecutive fiscal months;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the total of all taxes payable under Part III by the person and any person associated with the person in the fiscal year ending immediately before the particular fiscal year did not exceed $120,000;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the total of all taxes payable under Part III by the person and any person associated with the person in the particular fiscal year does not exceed $120,000; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the person is in compliance with this Act.

Subsection.Text: An authorization under subsection (2) is deemed to be revoked if the total of all taxes payable under Part III by the person and any person associated with the person exceeds $120,000 in a fiscal year, which revocation is effective as of the first day after the end of the fiscal half-year in which the excess occurs.
The Minister may revoke an authorization if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person requests in writing the Minister to do so;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person fails to comply with this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister considers that the authorization is no longer required.

Subsection.Text: If the Minister revokes an authorization under subsection (4), the Minister shall send a notice in writing of the revocation to the person and shall specify in the notice the fiscal month for which the revocation becomes effective.

If a revocation under subsection (4) becomes effective before the last day of a fiscal half-year of a person that is authorized under subsection (2), the period beginning on the first day of the fiscal half-year and ending immediately before the first day of the fiscal month for which the revocation becomes effective is deemed to be a reporting period of the person.

Section.Text: Subsections 79(1) to (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every person who is required to pay tax under Part III and every person who holds a licence granted under or in respect of that Part shall, not later than the last day of the first month after each reporting period of the person,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: file a return with the Minister, in the prescribed form and manner, for that reporting period;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: calculate, in the return, the total amount of the tax payable, if any, by the person for that reporting period, and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: pay that amount to the Receiver General.

Section.Text: Subparagraphs 82(2.2)(a)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if a notice of assessment in respect of the tax debt is sent to or served on the person after March 3, 2004, on the day that is 90 days after the day on which the notice is sent or served,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if no notice referred to in subparagraph (i) in respect of the tax debt was sent or served and the earliest day on which the Minister can commence an action to collect that tax debt is after March 3, 2004, on that earliest day, and

Section.Text: Section 95.1 of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Failure to file a return when required
Section.Text: Every person who fails to file a return for a reporting period as and when required under subsection 79(1) shall pay a penalty equal to the sum of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an amount equal to 1% of the total of all amounts each of which is an amount that is required to be paid for the reporting period and was not paid on the day on which the return was required to be filed, and

Section.Text: Subsection 104(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Except as otherwise provided in this Act, if a notice or other document is to be served on a person, other than the Minister or the Commissioner or the Tribunal, the notice or document shall be sent by registered or certified mail addressed to that person at their latest known address or be served personally on that person.
Subsection 106.1(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of this Act, a notice referred to in subsection 72(6), 81.13(1), 81.15(5) or 81.17(5) that is sent to a person is, in the absence of any evidence to the contrary, deemed to have been sent on the day appearing from the notice to be the date thereof, unless called into question by the Minister or by some person acting for him or Her Majesty.
Section 106.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

For the purposes of this Act, if a notice or other communication in respect of a person is made available in electronic format such that it can be read or perceived by a person or a computer system or other similar device, the notice or other communication is deemed to be sent to the person and received by the person on the date that an electronic message is sent, to the electronic address most recently provided before that date by the person to the Minister for the purposes of this subsection, informing the person that a notice or other communication requiring the person’s immediate attention is available in the person’s secure electronic account. A notice or other communication is considered to be made available if it is posted by the Minister in the person’s secure electronic account and the person has authorized that notices or other communications may be made available in this manner and has not before that date revoked that authorization in a manner specified by the Minister.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 224:

Section.MarginalNote: No action for collection of tax
Section.Text: No person, other than Her Majesty in right of Canada, may bring an action or proceeding against any person for acting in compliance or intended compliance with this Part by collecting an amount as or on account of tax.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to any action or proceeding that has not, on or before July 13, 2010, been finally determined by the tribunals or courts of competent jurisdiction.

Section.Text: Subsection 274(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If, with respect to a transaction, a notice of assessment, reassessment or additional assessment involving the application of subsection (2) with respect to the transaction has been sent to a person, any person (other than a person to whom such a notice has been sent) is entitled, within 180 days after the day of sending of the notice, to request in writing that the Minister make an assessment, a reassessment or an additional assessment, applying subsection (2) with respect to that transaction.

Section.Text: Subsection 274.2(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If, with respect to a transaction, a notice of assessment, reassessment or additional assessment involving the application of subsection (2) with respect to the transaction has been sent to a person, any person (other than a person to whom such a notice has been sent) is entitled, within 180 days after the day on which the notice was sent, to request in writing that the Minister make an assessment, a reassessment or an additional assessment, applying subsection (2) with respect to that transaction.

Section.Text: Subparagraphs 313(2.2)(a)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if a notice of assessment, or a notice referred to in subsection 322(1), in respect of the tax debt, is sent to or served on the person after March 3, 2004, on the last day on which one of those notices is sent or served,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if no notice referred to in subparagraph (i) in respect of the tax debt was sent or served and the earliest day on which the Minister can commence an action to collect that tax debt is after March 3, 2004, on that earliest day, and

Section.Text: Subsection 315(2) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If the Minister sends a notice of assessment to a person, any amount assessed then remaining unpaid is payable forthwith by the person to the Receiver General.

Section.Text: Subsections 335(10) and (11) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If any notice or demand that the Minister is required or authorized under this Part to send to a person is mailed, or sent electronically, to the person, the day of mailing or sending, as the case may be, shall be presumed to be the date of the notice or demand.

For the purposes of this Part, if a notice or other communication in respect of a person is made available in electronic format such that it can be read or perceived by a person or a computer system or other similar device, the notice or other communication is presumed to be sent to the person and received by the person on the date that an electronic message is sent, to the electronic address most recently provided before that date by the person to the Minister for the purposes of this subsection, informing the person that a notice or other communication requiring the person’s immediate attention is available in the person’s secure electronic account. A notice or other communication is considered to be made available if it is posted by the Minister in the person’s secure electronic account and the person has authorized that notices or other communications may be made available in this manner and has not before that date revoked that authorization in a manner specified by the Minister.
If a notice of assessment has been sent by the Minister as required under this Part, the assessment is deemed to have been made on the day of sending of the notice of assessment.

Section.Text: Section 7 of the Brewery Departmental Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The return required by section 175 of the Act shall be made

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a licensed brewer authorized by the Minister to make returns for six-month periods under subsection 36.1(2) of the Act, for each six-month period; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, for each month.

Subsection.Text: The return shall set out the following particulars:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the quantity of beer produced;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the quantity of beer exported;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the quantity of beer on which excise duty was paid that has been destroyed or returned to process stock; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of excise duty paid on beer.

Section.Text: Section 5 of the Brewery Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), the duty imposed under the Act in respect of beer produced during a particular month shall be paid not later than the last day of the month following the particular month.

If a licensed brewer is authorized by the Minister to make returns for six-month periods under subsection 36.1(2) of the Act, the duty imposed under the Act in respect of beer produced during a six-month period shall be paid not later than the last day of the month following the period.

Heading.TitleText: New Harmonized Value-added Tax System Regulations

Section.Text: Subsection 37(4) of the New Harmonized Value-added Tax System Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If, with respect to a transaction, a notice of assessment, reassessment or additional assessment involving the application of subsection (2) with respect to the transaction has been sent to a person, any person (other than a person to whom such a notice has been sent) is entitled, within 180 days after the day on which the notice was sent, to request in writing that the Minister make an assessment, a reassessment or an additional assessment, applying subsection (2) with respect to that transaction.

Heading.Label: PART 3
Heading.TitleText: AMENDMENTS TO THE FEDERAL-PROVINCIAL FISCAL ARRANGEMENTS ACT IN RESPECT OF INCOME TAX

Subsection.Text: The Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act is amended by adding the following after section 12.01:

Heading.Label: PART IV.01
Heading.TitleText: TRANSFER PAYMENTS — TAX IN RESPECT OF STOCK OPTION BENEFIT DEFERRAL UNDER PART I.01 OF THE INCOME TAX ACT

Section.Text: Subject to this Act, the Minister may pay to a province, out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, at such time as the Minister may determine, an amount equal to 1/3 of the tax payable under subparagraph 180.01(2)(c)(ii) of the Income Tax Act for a taxation year by a taxpayer who is resident in the province on the last day of that taxation year.

No payment may be made under this Part to a province if, in the opinion of the Minister, the province imposes or purports to impose a tax that is similar to the tax imposed under Part I.01 of the Income Tax Act.

Subsection.Text: Part IV.01 of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), is deemed to have come into force on on March 4, 2010.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 12.3:

Heading.Label: PART IV.2
Heading.TitleText: TRANSFER PAYMENTS — TAX ON PAYMENTS UNDER REGISTERED EDUCATION SAVINGS PLANS UNDER PART X.5 OF THE INCOME TAX ACT

Section.Text: Subject to this Act, the Minister may pay to a province, out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, at such time as the Minister may determine, an amount equal to 40% of the tax payable under Part X.5 of the Income Tax Act for a taxation year by a person who is resident in the province on the last day of that taxation year.

No payment may be made under this Part to a province if, in the opinion of the Minister, the province imposes or purports to impose a tax that is similar to the tax imposed under Part X.5 of the Income Tax Act.

Subsection.Text: Part IV.2 of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), applies in respect of the 1998 and subsequent taxation years.

Heading.Label: PART 4
Heading.TitleText: AMENDMENTS RELATING TO EXTERNAL COMPLAINTS BODIES

Section.Text: Section 2 of the Bank Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: organisme externe de traitement des plaintes

Definition.Text: external complaints body means a body corporate approved under subsection 455.01(1) or designated under subsection 455.1(1).

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 455:

Subsection.Text: Subject to section 455.1, the Minister may, on the Commissioner’s recommendation and for the purposes of this section, approve a body corporate incorporated under Part II of the Canada Corporations Act or under the Canada Business Corporations Act whose purpose, in the Minister’s view, under its letters patent is dealing with complaints, made by persons having requested or received products or services from its member financial institutions, that have not been resolved to the satisfaction of those persons under procedures established by those financial institutions under paragraph 455(1)(a).
A bank must be a member of one body corporate that is approved under subsection (1).

The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting the requirements for the approval of a body corporate under subsection (1) and the requirements for a body corporate approved under that subsection for maintaining that approval.
A body corporate approved under subsection (1) is not an agent of Her Majesty.
An approval given under subsection (1) must be published in the Canada Gazette.

A body corporate that is seeking an approval under subsection (1) must apply to the Commissioner, and the application must provide, in the form and manner required by the Commissioner, any information, material and evidence that he or she may require.
Section 455.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):
If the Minister makes a designation under subsection (1), any approval given under subsection 455.01(1) is revoked.

If the Minister makes a designation under subsection (1), the body corporate designated under that subsection must deal with any complaint that was made to a body corporate approved under subsection 455.01(1) and that has not been resolved.
Section 455.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):
The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting the requirements to be met by the body corporate designated under subsection (1).

Section.Text: Paragraph 459.5(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the persons who request or receive the product or service have access to complaint handling by an external complaints body.

Section.Text: Section 573.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:
An authorized foreign bank must be a member of one external complaints body.
Section 657 of the Act and the headings before it are replaced by the following:

Heading.Label: PART XIV
Heading.TitleText: REGULATION OF BANKS AND EXTERNAL COMPLAINTS BODIES — COMMISSIONER

Section.Text: A bank, authorized foreign bank or external complaints body must provide the Commissioner with the information at the times and in the form that he or she may require for the purposes of the administration of the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada Act and the consumer provisions.
Subsection 658(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), information regarding the business or affairs of a bank, authorized foreign bank or external complaints body or regarding persons dealing with any of them that is obtained by the Commissioner or by any person acting under the Commissioner’s direction, in the course of the exercise or performance of powers, duties and functions referred to in subsection 5(1) of the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada Act, and any information prepared from that information, is confidential and shall be treated accordingly.
Subsection 659(1) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Afin de s’assurer que la banque, la banque étrangère autorisée ou l’organisme externe de traitement des plaintes se conforme aux dispositions visant les consommateurs applicables, le commissaire, à l’occasion, mais au moins une fois par an, procède ou fait procéder à un examen et à une enquête dont il fait rapport au ministre.
Paragraph 659(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: has a right of access to any records, including electronic records, of a bank, authorized foreign bank or external complaints body; and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 659(2)(b) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: may require the directors or officers of a bank, authorized foreign bank or external complaints body to provide information and explanations, to the extent that they are reasonably able to do so, in respect of any matter subject to examination or inquiry under subsection (1).

Section.Text: Section 661 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Commissioner may enter into an agreement, called a “compliance agreement”, with a bank, authorized foreign bank or external complaints body for the purposes of implementing any measure that is designed so as to further compliance by it with the consumer provisions.
Section 974 of the Act is replaced by the following:

An instrument issued or made under this Act and directed to a single bank, bank holding company, authorized foreign bank or person, other than a regulation made under subsection 455.01(3) or 455.1(3.1) or an order referred to in section 499, is not a statutory instrument for the purposes of the Statutory Instruments Act.

Heading.TitleText: Financial Consumer Agency of Canada Act

Section.Text: Section 2 of the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: organisme externe de traitement des plaintes

Definition.Text: external complaints body has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Bank Act.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 3(2)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: supervise financial institutions and external complaints bodies to determine whether the institution or body is in compliance with

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the consumer provisions applicable to them, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the terms and conditions or undertakings with respect to the protection of customers of financial institutions that the Minister imposes or requires, as the case may be, under an Act listed in Schedule 1 and the directions that the Minister imposes under this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: promote the adoption by financial institutions and external complaints bodies of policies and procedures designed to implement the provisions, terms and conditions, undertakings or directions referred to in paragraph (a);

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: promote the adoption by financial institutions of policies and procedures designed to implement

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: voluntary codes of conduct that are designed to protect the interests of their customers, that are adopted by financial institutions and that are publicly available, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any public commitments made by them that are designed to protect the interests of their customers;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 3(2)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: promote consumer awareness about the obligations of financial institutions and of external complaints bodies under consumer provisions applicable to them and about all matters connected with the protection of consumers of financial products and services; and

Section.Text: Section 14 of the Act is replaced by the following:

No Commissioner, person appointed under subsection 4(4) or Deputy Commissioner shall beneficially own, directly or indirectly, any shares of any financial institution, bank holding company, insurance holding company, external complaints body or of any other body corporate, however created, carrying on any business in Canada that is substantially similar to any business carried on by any financial institution or external complaints body.
Subsection 16(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: No grant or gratuity to be made

Subsection.Text: The Commissioner, a person appointed under subsection 4(4), a Deputy Commissioner and any person appointed under section 10 shall not accept or receive, directly or indirectly, any grant or gratuity from a financial institution, bank holding company, insurance holding company, external complaints body, or from a director, officer or employee of any of them, and no such financial institution, bank holding company, insurance holding company, external complaints body, director, officer or employee shall make or give any such grant or gratuity.

Section.Text: Subsection 17(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2) and except as otherwise provided in this Act, information regarding the business or affairs of a financial institution or external complaints body or regarding persons dealing with one that is obtained by the Commissioner or by any person acting under the Commissioner’s direction, in the course of the exercise or performance of powers, duties and functions referred to in subsections 5(1) and (2) and 5.1(2), and any information prepared from that information, is confidential and shall be treated accordingly.
Subsection 18(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Commissioner shall, before December 31 in each year, ascertain the total amount of expenses incurred during the immediately preceding fiscal year for or in connection with the administration of this Act and the consumer provisions — excluding the expenses incurred in connection with the objects described in subsection 3(3) — and the amounts of any prescribed categories of those expenses in relation to any prescribed group of financial institutions and external complaints bodies.
Subsection 18(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

As soon as possible after ascertaining the amounts under subsections (1) and (1.1), the Commissioner shall assess a portion of the total amount of expenses against each financial institution and external complaints body to the extent and in the manner that the Governor in Council may, by regulation, prescribe.
Subsections 18(4) and (5) of the Act are replaced by the following:
The Commissioner may, during each fiscal year, prepare an interim assessment against any financial institution or external complaints body.
Every assessment and interim assessment is final and conclusive and binding on the financial institution or external complaints body against which it is made.

Section.Text: The portion of section 34 of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

The Minister shall cause to be laid before each House of Parliament, not later than the fifth sitting day of that House after September 30 next after the end of each fiscal year, a report showing the Agency’s operations for that year and describing

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in aggregate form, its conclusions on the compliance, in that year, of financial institutions and external complaints bodies with the consumer provisions applicable to them; and

Section.Text: On the first day on which both section 306 of the Canada Not-for-profit Corporations Act and section 146 of this Act are in force, subsection 455.01(1) of the Bank Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to section 455.1, the Minister may, on the Commissioner’s recommendation and for the purposes of this section, approve a body corporate incorporated under the Canada Not-for-profit Corporations Act or under the Canada Business Corporations Act whose purpose, in the Minister’s view, under its letters patent is dealing with complaints, made by persons having requested or received products or services from its member financial institutions, that have not been resolved to the satisfaction of those persons under procedures established by those financial institutions under paragraph 455(1)(a).
In this section, “other Act” means the Jobs and Economic Growth Act.
If section 2116 of the other Act comes into force before section 158 of this Act, then that section 158 is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Subsection 14(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: No Commissioner, person appointed under subsection 4(4) or Deputy Commissioner shall beneficially own, directly or indirectly, any shares of any financial institution, bank holding company, insurance holding company, external complaints body or of any other body corporate, however created, carrying on any business in Canada that is substantially similar to any business carried on by any financial institution or external complaints body.
If section 2116 of the other Act and section 158 of this Act come into force on the same day, then that section 158 is deemed to have come into force before that section 2116.

Section.Text: This Part, other than sections 163 and 164, comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Paragraph 2(2)(b) of the Canada Disability Savings Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the expressions contribution, designated provincial program, DTC-eligible individual, holder, issuer and registered disability savings plan have the same meanings as in section 146.4 of that Act; and

Subsection.Text: Section 6 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):
For the purposes of subsection (2), a contribution allocated to a year under subsection (2.2) is deemed to have been made in that year.

The Minister may allocate a contribution made to the beneficiary’s registered disability savings plan in a year after 2010, in parts — to the year in which it is actually made and to each of the previous 10 years that is after 2007 — in the following order:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: up to $500 to each year in which the beneficiary is one referred to in paragraph (2)(a), beginning with the earliest year, less any contributions allocated to the year in question;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: up to $1500 to each year in which the beneficiary is one referred to in paragraph (2)(a), beginning with the earliest year, less any contributions allocated to the year in question including those so allocated under paragraph (a); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: up to $1000 to each year in which the beneficiary is not one referred to in paragraph (2)(a), beginning with the earliest year, less any contributions allocated to the year in question.

Subsection.Text: No contribution made to the plan in a year may be allocated to a previous year unless, during that previous year, the beneficiary was resident in Canada and a DTC-eligible individual.

The Minister may allocate only the portion of contributions made to the plan in a year in respect of which, in accordance with subsection (2), a Canada Disability Savings Grant of up to $10,500 may be paid into the plan in that year.

For the purposes of determining the contributions allocated to the year in question under any of paragraphs (2.2)(a) to (c), contributions made to the plan in 2008, 2009 or 2010 are considered to have been allocated to the year in which they were actually made.
Section 6 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (7):
Not more than $10,500 in Canada Disability Savings Grants may be paid in respect of a beneficiary in a year.

Once a year, the Minister shall cause each holder of a registered disability savings plan to be provided with a statement that sets out the amount of Canada Disability Savings Grants that may be paid for particular years on the basis of future contributions.

Section.Text: Subsection 7(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to this Act and the regulations, on application, the Minister may pay a Canada Disability Savings Bond into a registered disability savings plan of a beneficiary

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for each year after the year in which the plan is entered into; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the year in which the plan is entered into and for each of the previous 10 years

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: that is after 2007,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: during which the beneficiary was resident in Canada, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: for which a Canada Disability Savings Bond has not previously been paid.

Subsection.Text: A Canada Disability Savings Bond is to be paid on any terms and conditions that the Minister may specify by agreement between the Minister and the issuer of the plan.

Section.Text: For the purposes of calculating the amount of a Canada Disability Savings Grant that may be paid for 2008 under section 6 of the Canada Disability Savings Act, subparagraphs 6(2)(a)(i) and (ii) and subsection 6(6) of that Act are to be read as they did on December 31, 2008.

For the purposes of calculating the amount of a Canada Disability Savings Bond that may be paid for 2008 under section 7 of the Canada Disability Savings Act, subparagraphs 7(2)(a)(i) and (ii) and (b)(i) and (ii), the descriptions of B and C in subsection 7(4) and subsection 7(8) of that Act are to be read as they did on December 31, 2008.
This Part comes into force, or is deemed to have come into force, on January 1, 2011.
Section 11.1 of the Customs Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Subsection.Text: The User Fees Act does not apply to a fee for an authorization issued under this section if it is a reciprocal fee under an international arrangement.
Subsections 6(1) to (6) of the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act are replaced by the following:
Subject to subsections (8) to (10), the fiscal stabilization payment that may be paid to a province for a fiscal year is the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the non-natural resource revenue of the province for the preceding fiscal year;
is the non-natural resource revenue of the province for the fiscal year;
is equal to

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 95%, if the natural resource revenue of the province for the fiscal year is greater than the natural resource revenue of the province for the preceding fiscal year,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 50%, if the natural resource revenue of the province for the fiscal year is less than one half of the natural resource revenue of the province for the preceding fiscal year, or

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the natural resource revenue of the province for the preceding fiscal year;
is equal to

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: one, if the natural resource revenue of the province for the fiscal year is either greater than the natural resource revenue of the province for the preceding fiscal year or less than one half of the natural resource revenue of the province for the preceding fiscal year, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: zero, in any other case; and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the natural resource revenue of the province for the fiscal year.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of determining the amount under subsection (1), the Minister may adjust, in the prescribed manner, the natural resource revenue and the non-natural resource revenue of a province for the fiscal year to offset the amount, as determined by the Minister, of any change in either of those revenues for the fiscal year resulting from changes made by the province in the rates or structure of provincial taxes or of other means of raising the revenue of the province from the rates or structures in effect in the preceding fiscal year.

If a province has entered into a tax collection agreement respecting personal income tax or corporation income tax, a change to the Income Tax Act affecting the province’s Common Tax Base as defined in Chapter 2 or 3, as the case may be, of the tax collection agreement is deemed to be a change in the rates or in the structures of provincial taxes for the purposes of subsection (2).

For the purposes of determining the amount under subsection (1), the natural resource revenue of a province for a fiscal year is the aggregate of the total revenue, as determined by the Minister, derived by the province for the fiscal year from the revenue sources described in paragraphs (l) to (w) of the definition revenue source in subsection 3.9(1) and from the revenue sources described in paragraph (z.5) of that definition that relate to natural resources.
For the purposes of determining the amount under subsection (1), the non-natural resource revenue of a province for a fiscal year is equal to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the aggregate of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the total revenue, as determined by the Minister, derived by the province for the fiscal year from the revenue sources described in paragraphs (a) to (k), (x), (y) and (z.1) to (z.3) of the definition revenue source in subsection 3.9(1),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the total revenue, as determined by the Minister, derived by the province for the fiscal year from the revenue sources described in paragraph (z.5) of that definition that do not relate to natural resources,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the fiscal equalization payment made to the province for the fiscal year under Part I, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the additional cash payment made to the province for the fiscal year under section 24.703,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: despite subsection (5), the aggregate of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the total equalized tax transfer applicable to the province as if, for that fiscal year, the transfer were determined in the manner described in subsection 24.7(1.22), and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the value of the additional tax abatement units as determined in accordance with subsection 27(2).

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of determining the non-natural resource revenue of a province for a fiscal year,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 3.9(2) applies, with any modifications that the circumstances require, in determining the revenue derived by the province for the fiscal year from personal income taxes, described in paragraph (a) of the definition revenue source in subsection 3.9(1), except that no deduction may be made in respect of the tax abatement units referred to in subsection 27(2);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the province’s revenue for the fiscal year derived from personal income taxes, described in paragraph (a) of the definition revenue source in subsection 3.9(1), is deemed to be

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the total amount, determined in the prescribed manner, of the provincial personal income taxes assessed or reassessed, not later than 24 months after the end of the fiscal year, in respect of the taxation year ending in the fiscal year,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the total amount, determined in the prescribed manner, of the tax credits and rebates claimed by taxpayers in the province against provincial personal income taxes for that taxation year that have been deducted from those assessed or reassessed provincial personal income taxes; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the province’s revenue for the fiscal year derived from that part of the revenue source described in paragraph (b) of the definition revenue source in subsection 3.9(1) that consists of corporation income taxes is deemed to be

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the total amount, determined in the prescribed manner, of the provincial corporation income taxes assessed or reassessed, not later than 24 months after the end of the fiscal year, in respect of the taxation year of corporations ending in the calendar year that ends in the fiscal year,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the total amount, determined in the prescribed manner, of the tax credits and rebates claimed by taxpayers in the province against provincial corporation income taxes for that taxation year that have been deducted from those assessed or reassessed provincial corporation income taxes.

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (4), for the purposes of determining the amount under subsection (1) for the fiscal year that begins on April 1, 2011, the payment under section 3.12 and the additional cash payment under section 24.703 are not included in the province’s non-natural resource revenue.
Subsection 6(11) of the Act is repealed.

Heading.Label: PART 8
Heading.TitleText: OFFICE OF THE SUPERINTENDENT OF FINANCIAL INSTITUTIONS ACT

Section.Text: The heading before section 23 of the Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subsection 23(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
In every fiscal year, the Superintendent shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: estimate the total amount of expenses to be incurred by the Office during the following fiscal year for or in connection with the administration of the Pension Benefits Standards Act, 1985; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: ascertain the total amount of expenses incurred by the Office during the preceding fiscal year for or in connection with the administration of the Pension Benefits Standards Act, 1985.

Subsection.Text: Any amount ascertained or estimated by the Superintendent under subsection (1) or (1.1), as the case may be, is final and conclusive for the purposes of this section.
Section 23 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):
In every fiscal year, the Superintendent shall assess, in the prescribed manner, an amount against the administrator of a pension plan. The amount is to be determined as prescribed.
The determination of the amount referred to in subsection (5) shall take into account the amounts referred to in subsection (1.1).

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 24(1) of the Act before the definition entity is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The following definitions apply in this section and in sections 25 to 37.2.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 37:

Subsection.Text: The Superintendent may remit all or part of any assessment or interim assessment made under section 23 or 23.1 or any penalty imposed under this Act, including any interest on that assessment, interim assessment or penalty.
A remission may be conditional or unconditional.

The Superintendent may write off from the accounts of the Office all or part of any debt referred to in subsection 23.2(2) or 31(1) that has been determined to be uncollectible or for which further administrative expense or other costs of collecting the debt are not justifiable in relation to the amount of the debt or the probability of collection.
The writing-off of any debt does not affect any right of Her Majesty to collect or recover the debt.
The regulations made under subsection 25(1) of the Financial Administration Act do not apply to the writing off of a debt under this section.

Section.Text: Sections 174 and 175 come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Subsection.Text: The definition designated province in subsection 2(1) of the Pension Benefits Standards Act, 1985 is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: designated province means a province prescribed as a province in which there is in force pension legislation applicable to private superannuation plans;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: electronic document means any form of representation of information or of concepts fixed in any medium by electronic, optical or other similar means that can be read or perceived by a person or by any means;
information system means a system used to generate, send, receive, store or otherwise process an electronic document;
multilateral agreement means an agreement entered into under subsection 6.1(1);

negotiated contribution plan means a multi-employer pension plan that includes at least one defined benefit provision and under which a participating employer’s contributions are limited to an amount determined in accordance with an agreement entered into by the participating employers or a collective agreement, statute or regulation, and which amount does not vary as a function of the prescribed tests and standards for solvency referred to in subsection 9(1);

Subsection.Text: Section 2 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

Even though a pension plan — established as a negotiated contribution plan — is no longer a negotiated contribution plan because it has only one participating employer or more than 95% of its members are employed by participating employers who are incorporated and are affiliates within the meaning of the Canada Business Corporations Act, that pension plan is deemed to be a negotiated contribution plan for a period of one year after the day it is no longer a negotiated contribution plan, or for any longer period that may be specified by the Superintendent.

Section.Text: Subsection 5(2) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (b), by adding “and’’ at the end of paragraph (c) and by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: collect information from a pension supervisory authority of a designated province and disclose information to that authority for the purposes of implementing a multilateral agreement.

Section.Text: Section 6 of the Act and the heading before it are replaced by the following:
The Minister may, with the approval of the Governor in Council, enter into a bilateral agreement with a designated province to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: authorize the pension supervisory authority of that province to exercise any of the Superintendent’s powers under this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: authorize the Superintendent to exercise any powers of that pension supervisory authority.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, with the approval of the Governor in Council, enter into an agreement with two or more designated provinces respecting any matter relating to pension plans that are subject to the pension legislation of at least one designated province that is a party to the agreement.
A multilateral agreement may, among other things,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: limit the application of the pension legislation of a designated province that is a party to the agreement to a pension plan and adapt that legislation to that pension plan;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: limit the application of this Act and the regulations to a pension plan and adapt them to that pension plan;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: exempt a pension plan from the application of this Act and the regulations or the pension legislation of a designated province that is a party to the agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: provide for the administration and enforcement of this Act, the regulations and the pension legislation of a designated province that is a party to the agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: authorize a pension supervisory authority of a designated province that is a party to the agreement or the association referred to in section 6.4 to exercise any of the Superintendent’s powers under this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: authorize the Superintendent to exercise any powers of a pension supervisory authority of a designated province that is a party to the agreement or the association referred to in section 6.4;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: establish requirements — in addition to any other requirements under this Act, the regulations and the pension legislation of a designated province that is a party to the agreement — with respect to a pension plan, administrator or employer; and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: confer powers on the Superintendent.

Subsection.Text: The Minister must cause to be tabled in each House of Parliament every multilateral agreement.
The Minister must cause to be published in the Canada Gazette

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: every multilateral agreement and a notice of the date on which the agreement comes into effect with respect to pension plans;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: every amendment to a multilateral agreement and a notice of the date on which the amendment comes into effect with respect to pension plans; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a notice of the effective date of the Government of Canada’s withdrawal from the multilateral agreement or of the effective date of termination of that agreement, whichever comes first.
