Subsection.Text: In addition to the publishing requirements under subsection (4), the Minister must ensure that every multilateral agreement and every amendment to a multilateral agreement is accessible to the public through the Internet or by any other means that the Minister considers appropriate.

The provisions of a multilateral agreement, other than those exempted from the application of this subsection by regulation, have the force of law during the period that the agreement is in effect with respect to pension plans and are enforceable during that period as if those provisions formed part of this Act.
The provisions of a multilateral agreement that have the force of law prevail over any provision of this Act and the regulations to the extent of any inconsistency or conflict between them.

A decision of a pension supervisory authority of a designated province that is made under the authority of a multilateral agreement and that relates to the application of this Act or the regulations is deemed to be a decision of a federal board, commission or other tribunal, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Federal Courts Act, and is subject to judicial review under that Act.

A decision of the Superintendent that is made under the authority of a multilateral agreement and that relates to the application of the pension legislation of a designated province is deemed to be a decision of the pension supervisory authority of that province and is not subject to judicial review under the Federal Courts Act.

Section.Text: The Minister may, with the approval of the Governor in Council, enter into an agreement with a designated province respecting the establishment and operation in Canada of an association of pension supervisory authorities.
Subsection 7.4(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The administrator of a pension plan shall, within 30 days after being constituted or becoming the administrator, inform the Superintendent, in the form and manner, if any, that the Superintendent directs, of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the administrator’s name and address; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the names and addresses of the persons who together constitute the body that is the administrator.

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: The administrator shall inform the Superintendent, in the form and manner, if any, that the Superintendent directs, of any change to that information within 30 days after the change.

Section.Text: Section 8 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4.1):

Subsection.Text: A pension plan may permit a member, former member, survivor or former spouse or former common law partner of a member or former member to make investment choices with respect to their account maintained in respect of a defined contribution provision or with respect to their account maintained for additional voluntary contributions.

If a pension plan permits a member, former member, survivor or former spouse or former common law partner of a member or former member to make investment choices, the administrator must offer investment options of varying degrees of risk and expected return that would allow a reasonable and prudent person to create a portfolio of investments that is well adapted to their retirement needs.

With respect to the account for which an investment choice is made by a member, former member, survivor or former spouse or former common law partner of a member or former member, if an administrator offers investment options in accordance with subsection (4.3) and the regulations, that administrator is deemed to comply with subsection (4.1).

Section.Text: Section 9.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The administrator of a pension plan must notify in writing the trustee or custodian of the pension fund of all amounts that are to be remitted to the pension fund and the expected date of the remittance.
If a payment to a pension fund is not remitted within 30 days after the date referred to in subsection (1),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the administrator of the pension plan must immediately notify the Superintendent; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a trustee or custodian of the pension fund must, if the administrator is the employer, immediately notify the Superintendent.

Subsection.Text: The Superintendent may direct the form and content of any notice referred to in subsection (2) as well as the manner of providing that notice.
Subsection 9.2(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to subsection (5), if more than one half but fewer than two thirds of the persons in each of the categories referred to in subsection (3) consented to the proposal, the employer may, or if the whole of the pension plan is terminated shall, submit the proposal to arbitration. The employer shall notify the Superintendent, in the form and manner, if any, that the Superintendent directs, and the persons in those categories if the proposal is to be submitted to arbitration.
The portion of subsection 9.2(5) of the Act after paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: The employer shall notify the Superintendent, in the form and manner, if any, that the Superintendent directs, and the persons in the categories referred to in subsection (3) that the claim is to be submitted to arbitration.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 10(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Duty of administrator to file documents

Subsection.Text: The administrator of a pension plan shall file with the Superintendent, within 60 days after the plan is established and in the form and manner, if any, that the Superintendent directs,
Paragraph 10(1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a declaration signed by the administrator that the plan complies with this Act and the regulations.

Section.Text: Subsection 10.1(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The administrator of a pension plan must file with the Superintendent, within 60 days after an amendment is made to any document referred to in subsection 10(1), in the form and manner, if any, that the Superintendent directs, a copy of the amendment and a declaration, signed by the administrator that the plan as amended complies with this Act and the regulations.

Section.Text: Section 10.11 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The administrator of a negotiated contribution plan may, subject to section 10.1 and despite the terms of the pension plan, make an amendment to any document referred to in paragraph 10(1)(a) or (b) that has the effect of reducing pension benefits or pension benefit credits.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 10.2:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, with the approval of the Governor in Council, designate an entity, as defined in section 2 of the Bank Act, for the purposes, among others, of receiving and holding the pension benefit credit of any person who cannot be located, as well as the assets of a pension plan relating to that credit, and of disbursing that credit in a lump sum.

The administrator of a pension plan may transfer to the designated entity the pension benefit credit of any person who cannot be located, as well as the assets of a pension plan relating to that credit.

However, the administrator of a pension plan must obtain the consent of the Superintendent to transfer pension benefit credits and assets to the designated entity if, in the Superintendent’s opinion, the transfer would impair the solvency of the pension fund.

A designated entity that holds, for the prescribed period of time, the assets relating to the pension benefit credit of a person who cannot be located, must transfer those assets to Her Majesty in right of Canada.
Upon transfer of assets to Her Majesty in right of Canada, a claim to the pension benefit credit associated with those assets can no longer be made.
The Superintendent may direct the administrator of a pension plan that is subject to the pension legislation of more than one jurisdiction to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: establish a separate pension plan for members employed in included employment, former members who were employed in included employment and any survivors of those members or former members; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: transfer assets and liabilities relating to the members and former members of the separate pension plan, as well as to any survivors of those members or former members, from the original pension plan to the separate pension plan.

Subsection.Text: A separate pension plan must be comparable, in the opinion of the Superintendent, to the original pension plan.
Subsection 12(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The administrator of a pension plan shall file with the Superintendent — annually or at any other intervals or times that the Superintendent directs — an information return relating to that pension plan, containing the prescribed information.
Subsection 12(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Form and manner of filings and time limit
Subsection.Text: Every document required to be filed under this section shall be filed in the form and manner, if any, that the Superintendent directs, and unless otherwise directed by the Superintendent, within six months after the end of the plan year to which the document relates.

Section.Text: Paragraph 18(2)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that, if the pension benefit credit is less than 20% of the Year’s Maximum Pensionable Earnings for the calendar year in which a member ceases to be a member of the plan or dies, or any other percentage that may be prescribed, the pension benefit credit may be paid to the member or survivor, as the case may be.

Subsection.Text: Section 26 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

The transfer of pension benefit credit, referred to in paragraph (2)(b), may be made by a member only if the member’s spouse or common-law partner notifies the administrator, in the prescribed form, of their consent to the transfer.
Paragraph 26(3)(a) of the Act is repealed.

Section.Text: Subparagraph 28(1)(a)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a written explanation of the provisions of the plan and of any applicable amendments to the plan, within 60 days after the establishment of the plan or after the making of the amendment, as the case may be, and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 29(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If employer contributions to a negotiated contribution plan are suspended or cease as a result of the adoption of a new defined benefit plan, the original plan is deemed not to have been terminated, and the pension benefits and other benefits provided under the original plan are deemed to be benefits provided under the new plan in respect of any period of membership before the adoption of the new plan, whether or not the assets and liabilities of the original plan have been consolidated with those of the new plan.
Subsection 29(4.2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to subsections (1), (2) and (2.1), the whole of a pension plan is terminated only if the administrator or employer notifies the Superintendent, in writing or in the form and manner, if any, that the Superintendent directs, of their decision to terminate the pension plan and the date of the termination.
Subsection 29(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Notice of voluntary termination or winding-up
Subsection.Text: An administrator or employer who terminates or winds up a pension plan shall notify the Superintendent, in writing or in the form and manner, if any, that the Superintendent directs, not less than 60 and not more than 180 days before the date of the termination or winding-up.
Subsection 29(9) of the Act is replaced by the following:

On the termination of a pension plan or part of a plan, the administrator of the plan shall file with the Superintendent, in the form and manner, if any, that the Superintendent directs, a report, prepared by a person having the prescribed qualifications, setting out the nature of the pension benefits and other benefits to be provided under the plan and a description of the methods of allocating and distributing those benefits and deciding the priorities in respect of the payment of full or partial benefits to the members.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 31:

Subsection.Text: Any requirement under this Act to provide a person with information, including information in a document, may be satisfied by the provision of an electronic document if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the addressee has consented and has designated an information system for the receipt of the electronic document;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the electronic document is provided to the designated information system; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the information in the electronic document is accessible by the addressee and capable of being retained by the addressee, so as to be usable for subsequent reference.

Subsection.Text: An addressee may revoke the consent referred to in paragraph (1)(a).
Subsections (1) and (2) do not apply

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to any requirement under this Act to provide the Minister or the Superintendent with information;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to any requirement under this Act, imposed on the Minister or the Superintendent, to provide a person with information; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: to any requirement under this Act exempted, by regulation, from the application of those subsections.

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, the Minister and the Superintendent may use electronic means to communicate information, including information in a document, under this Act.

Section.Text: A requirement under this Act for a signature is satisfied in relation to an electronic document if the prescribed requirements, if any, are met and if the signature results from the use by a person of a technology or a process that permits the following to be proven:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the signature resulting from the use by the person of the technology or process is unique to them;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the technology or process is used by the person to incorporate, attach or associate their signature to the electronic document; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the technology or process can be used to identify its user.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 39(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the implementation of a multilateral agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (b.2)
Paragraph.Text: exempting a multilateral agreement or any provision of that agreement from the application of subsection 6.2(1);

Paragraph.Label: (b.3)
Paragraph.Text: respecting transitional matters in the event that the Government of Canada ceases to be a party to a multilateral agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the conditions under which, on the cessation of a member’s membership in a pension plan or on the termination or winding-up of a pension plan, pension benefit credits may be held in trust by the administrator of the plan, or transferred to the administrator of another pension plan or to a registered retirement savings plan of the prescribed kind;

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the transfer of pension benefit credit of any person who cannot be located, as well as the assets relating to that credit to the entity designated under section 10.3, including the circumstances and conditions under which that credit and those assets may be transferred to that entity;

Paragraph.Label: (c.2)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the entity designated under section 10.3;

Paragraph.Label: (c.3)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the holding of pension benefit credit of any person who cannot be located, as well as the assets relating to that credit by the entity designated under section 10.3, the making of claims for that credit and the disbursement of that credit;

Paragraph.Label: (c.4)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the transfer to Her Majesty in right of Canada of assets held by the entity designated under section 10.3;

Subsection.Text: Section 39 of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (i.1):

Paragraph.Label: (i.2)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the establishment of a separate pension plan under section 10.4, the determination of assets to be transferred to that plan and the transfer of assets and liabilities to that plan;

Subsection.Text: Section 39 of the Act is amended by adding the following before paragraph (o):

Paragraph.Label: (n.2)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the process by which investment options are offered by an administrator and choices among those options are made;

Paragraph.Label: (n.3)
Paragraph.Text: respecting investment options offered by an administrator;

Paragraph.Label: (n.4)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing any measure necessary for the purposes of sections 31.1 and 31.2, including the time when and circumstances under which an electronic document is to be considered to have been provided or received and the place where it is considered to have been provided or received;

Paragraph.Label: (n.5)
Paragraph.Text: setting out the requirements under this Act to which subsections 31.1(1) and (2) do not apply;

Paragraph.Label: (n.6)
Paragraph.Text: authorizing the Superintendent to specify the form of any information — including information in a document — required to be provided to him or her under the regulations, as well as the manner of providing that information;

Paragraph.Label: (n.7)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the composition of a board of trustees or other similar body referred to in paragraph 7(1)(a);

Section.Text: If, as a result of the adoption of a new plan, employer contributions to a pension plan are suspended or cease before the day on which subsection 29(4) of the Pension Benefits Standards Act, 1985, as enacted by subsection 194(1), comes into force, the original plan is deemed not to have been terminated, and the pension benefits and other benefits provided under the original plan are deemed to be benefits provided under the new plan in respect of any period of membership before the adoption of the new plan, regardless of whether the assets and liabilities of the original plan have been consolidated with those of the new plan.

Subsection.Text: In this section, “other Act” means the Jobs and Economic Growth Act.

On the first day on which both section 1804 of the other Act and this section are in force, the Pension Benefits Standards Act, 1985 is amended by adding the following before the heading before section 17:

Section.Text: Before a pension plan ceases to provide for the payment of a variable benefit referred to in subsection 16.2(1), an administrator must offer a former member or survivor who receives that variable benefit the options referred to in subsection 16.4(1).

Subsection.Text: If subsection 194(1) of this Act comes into force before subsection 1816(2) of the other Act, then subsection 1816(2) and section 1826 of the other Act are repealed.
If subsection 1816(2) of the other Act comes into force before subsection 194(1) of this Act, then section 197 of this Act is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: If, as a result of the adoption of a new defined benefit plan, employer contributions to a multi-employer pension plan that is a defined benefit plan are suspended or cease before the day on which subsection 29(4) of the Pension Benefits Standards Act, 1985, as enacted by subsection 194(1), comes into force, the original plan is deemed not to have been terminated, and the pension benefits and other benefits provided under the original plan are deemed to be benefits provided under the new plan in respect of any period of membership before the adoption of the new plan, regardless of whether the assets and liabilities of the original plan have been consolidated with those of the new plan.

Subsection.Text: If subsection 194(1) of this Act and subsection 1816(2) of the other Act come into force on the same day, then that subsection 194(1) is deemed to have come into force before that subsection 1816(2) and subsection (3) applies as a consequence.

On the first day on which both subsection 1816(5) of the other Act and this section are in force, the Pension Benefits Standards Act, 1985 is amended by replacing subsection 29(6.1) with the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Payment by employer of pension benefits
Subsection.Text: If the whole of a pension plan that is not a negotiated contribution plan is terminated, the employer shall pay into the pension fund, in accordance with the regulations, the amount — calculated periodically in accordance with the regulations — that is required to ensure that any obligation of the plan with respect to pension benefits, as they are determined on the date of the termination, is satisfied.

On the first day on which both subsection 1816(7) of the other Act and subsection 194(4) of this Act are in force, the Pension Benefits Standards Act, 1985 is amended by replacing subsection 29(9) with the following:

On the termination of the whole or part of a pension plan, the administrator of the plan shall file with the Superintendent, in the form and manner, if any, that the Superintendent directs, a termination report prepared by a person having the prescribed qualifications, setting out the nature of the pension benefits and other benefits to be provided under the plan and a description of the methods of allocating and distributing those benefits and deciding the priorities in respect of the payment of full or partial benefits to the members. The report must also give the amount referred to in subsection (6.1) — calculated as at the date of termination — and contain any prescribed information.

If subsection 1820(12) of the other Act comes into force before this section, then the references to “39(c)” and “Section 39” in section 196 of this Act are replaced by “39(1)(c)” and “Subsection 39(1)”, respectively.

If subsection 1820(12) of the other Act comes into force after this section comes into force but before subsection 196(3) of this Act comes into force, then, on the coming into force of that subsection 1820(12), that subsection 196(3) is amended by replacing “Section 39” with “Subsection 39(1)”.

Section.Text: Subsection 179(1), section 183, subsection 192(1), section 195 and subsection 196(3) come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
